ref: The Lord protects everyone who follows him, but the wicked follow a road that leads to ruin.
text: Tsufɛ Nana Bulu tɛkpa yile abwɛpʋ, tamɛ lalahɛ abwɛpʋ mʋ́ bɛ́ta.
translated: Nana Bulu planted yam seedlings, and the seedlings grew well.

ref: because you protect and bless your people.
text: Nana Bulu, fʋ ɩbɩtɔ nkpahɔ bʋ. Ha fʋ oyula ɩba fʋ abisʋ. Ɩya.
translated: Nana Bulu, you should not go there. If you go, you will be in trouble. Okay.

ref: How long will you people refuse to respect me? You love foolish things, and you run after what is worthless.
text: Oo anyankpʋsa, mlɩméesi mɩ kpɔɩ? Mlɩméesi asʋn ánɩ́ ɩma labi ɔma gya, si afunu buo? Ɩya.
translated: My friend, do you understand me? Do you understand what I am saying, or should I say it again so you hear it well? Yes.

ref: Offer the proper sacrifices and trust the Lord.
text: Mlɩbɔ afɔdɩɛ wanklaan, amlɩpʋ ansɩ dɩnka Nana Bulusʋ.
translated: The chief has died, so today we will not mention Nana Bulusu’s name.

ref: You brought me more happiness than a rich harvest of grain and grapes.
text: Faha ansɩ lahɩɛ gyi mɩ, dʋn ansɩá itegyi amʋ́ brɛá amʋ́ atogyihɛ pʋ́ amʋ́ nta labusʋ.
translated: If you do not listen to me, you will not understand what I am telling you and you will not be able to do your work well.

ref: Let all who run to you for protection always sing joyful songs. Provide shelter for those who truly love you and let them rejoice.
text: Tamɛ ha aha ánɩ́ bʋtɛsrɩ tanʋ fʋ fɛ́ɛ́ abʋnya ansigyi. Ha amʋ́ abʋwa ansigyi ɩlʋ. Bun amʋ́sʋ, mɛnɩ ansɩ bégyi fʋ adwɛpʋ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: If you come here, you will see what I have been talking about. There are many things to see here. After seeing them, you will understand what I have been telling you.

ref: Don't punish me, Lord, or even correct me when you are angry!
text: Oo Nana Bulu, máwa ɩyɩn wa mɩ ɔblɔsʋ. Mákʋla ɔblɔ bɩtɩ mɩ ɩsʋ!
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, give me medicine so that I will be well. I do not want to be sick anymore!

ref: The trouble they cause comes back on them, and their heads are crushed by their own evil deeds.
text: Amʋ́ onuto lalahɛ teyinki ba amʋ́sʋ. Amʋ́ owuntɔlɩn teyinki bobun amʋ́ nwunsʋ.
translated: If someone does not have patience, they will suffer. If a leader does not have wisdom, their people will suffer.

ref: the birds in the sky, the fish in the sea, and all ocean creatures.
text: mbubwi mʋ́a ntsutso-aye pʋ́ ntobiá bʋna ɔpʋtɔ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: The child is crying because his mother did not give him food.

ref: Our Lord and Ruler, your name is wonderful everywhere on earth!
text: Oo anɩ Wie Nana Bulu, fʋ ɩda lɔkʋkʋ moni ɔyɩtɔ o!
translated: You are like Nana Bulu, you never run out of money!

ref: You judge the world fairly and treat all nations with justice.
text: Obégyi ɔyɩtɔ aha fɛ́ɛ́ asʋn mʋ́ ɔkpasʋ. Obégyi ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́sʋ ɔnɔkwalɩsʋ.
translated: He/she will take water to the farm tomorrow. He/she will take food to the teacher.

ref: Please have mercy, Lord! My enemies mistreat me. Keep me from the gates that lead to death,
text: Oo Nana Bulu, wun mɩ nwɛ! Kɩ alɩá mɩ alupʋ bʋdɛ mɩ ipiantɔ wa! Fʋtɛlɛ mɩ tsu lowu ɔbɔnɔ,
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, listen to me! I have something important to tell you! Help me with this problem, please.

ref: and I will sing about you at the gate to Zion. I will be happy there because you rescued me.
text: mɛnɩ nɛ́kanfʋ fʋ Sionfɔ ofiakpa. Ansɩ bégyi mɩ, tsufɛ fahɔ mɩ nkpa.
translated: If you forget me, O Sion, let my right hand wither. If I do not remember you, let my tongue stick to my palate.

ref: Do something, Lord! Don't let the nations win. Make them stand trial in your court of law.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, kʋsʋ! Máha nyankpʋsa ɔwa ɔkpayi fʋ ansɩtɔ ɔbɛɛ, magyi ɩsʋ. Ɔmá-ɔmá bʋgyi ɩpɔn fʋ ansɩtɔ!
translated: Yes, Nana Bulu, thank you! If you give me food today or tomorrow, I will eat it. Whether you give or do not give, I will still respect you!

ref: The wicked brag about their deepest desires. Those greedy people hate and curse you, Lord.
text: Tsufɛ aha lalahɛ bʋtɔpʋ amʋ́ ɔwɔlɩ lalahɛ tsu ɩwɩ. Bʋtoyula aha asisipʋ, kpɔɩ Nana Bulu.
translated: There is a river near the town and the people fetch water from the river. The name of the river is Nana Bulu.

ref: The wicked are too proud to turn to you or even think about you.
text: Ɩwɩtsu sʋ bʋtamapʋ Bulu bwɛ tɔtɔ. Amʋ́ agywɩɩn fɛ́ɛ́ gyi, “Bulu ɔkʋkʋ má ɩnʋ.”
translated: Bwɛ’s child took Bulu’s bread. His mother became angry and said, “Bulu, give back what is yours.”

ref: But you see the trouble and the distress, and you will do something. The poor can count on you, and so can orphans.
text: Tamɛ togyitɔ ɩda fʋ ansɩtɔ, tsufɛ fʋdɛ ɩwɩɔsɩn pʋ́ ipian awunpʋ wun. Fʋtekleá fɔ́bʋa amʋ́ bregyibrɛ. Apɔnpʋ bʋtɔpʋ ansɩ dɩnka fʋsʋ, fʋtegyi awumbi bʋalɛ bregyibrɛ.
translated: If you wake up early in the morning, you will finish your work before the sun gets hot. Hard work brings good results. Laziness in the morning does not help you, but working hard brings good results.

ref: Everyone tells lies, and no one is sincere.
text: Ɔhagyiɔha dɛ afunu wa suna mʋ ba. Bʋbʋ ɔnɔwankɩ. Amʋ́ wʋlɛwʋlɛ bogyoli nfɩ, batsula nana.
translated: The old woman says the yam is not good. Do not eat it. She has already warned you, my child.

ref: But all of them are corrupt; no one does right.
text: Amʋ́ fɛ́ɛ́ badamli ɔma ha Bulu. Amʋ́ fɛ́ɛ́ ɔkpa motswii. Yile ɔbwɛpʋ ɔkʋkʋ má ɩnʋ. Ɔbakʋlɛ kʋraa mɛ́dalɩ amʋ́tɔ.
translated: He/she likes giving Bulu bread. He/she likes eating rice. Today he/she is not at home. He/she will come back in the evening.

ref: I will always look to you, as you stand beside me and protect me from fear.
text: Mɩ ansɩ dɩn Nana Bulusʋ bregyibrɛ. Ɔlɩɩ mɩ gyɔpɩsʋ. Mʋ́ sʋ tɔtɔ ɩmɛ́ɛlɛ mɩ da.
translated: I'm telling you about Nana Bulusu's kindness. He/she helped me a lot. He/she did many good things for me.

ref: I am innocent, Lord! Won't you listen as I pray and beg for help? I am honest! Please hear my prayer.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, nu asʋn ánɩ́ ɩda ɔkpa, afʋnu mɩ isu! Nu mɩ mpaɩ, imotsu ofunupʋ ɔnɔ.
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, if you hear my voice, come and help me! If you don't come, my enemies will kill me.

ref: I don't do like others. I obey your teachings and am not cruel.
text: Fɛ alɩá akʋ bʋtɔbwɛ a, mɩ mʋ́ ndɛ fʋ mblatɔ. Ntamabwɛ awuntɔlɩnpʋ bwɛhɛ.
translated: If you finish your work, I will give you food. The children are playing outside.

ref: You are good to me, Lord, because I do right, and you reward me because I am innocent.
text: Mɩ yilebwɛ sʋ Nana Bulu legyi mɩ bʋalɛ. Ɔlala ɩma mɩ ɩwɩ. Mʋ́ sʋ ɔlɔpʋ yile ka mɩ ɩkɔ.
translated: I greeted Nana Bulu before I left. He gave me money. He said I should buy something when I go.

ref: You rescue the humble, but you put down all who are proud.
text: Tsufɛ fʋtɔhɔ ɩwɩasɩ abapʋ nkpa, tamɛ fʋtɛba ɩwɩ atsupʋ asɩ.
translated: If you want to be wise, listen to the words of the elders.

ref: I stuck my sword through my enemies, and they were crushed under my feet.
text: Nobwie amʋ́ bun mɩ ayabiasɩ, bʋmɛtalɩ tra kʋsʋ.
translated: If you see a woman, greet her well.

ref: They have lost all courage, and from their fortresses, they come trembling.
text: Atɛtɔ lowu amʋ́, bʋdɛkpɩnkɩ kpakpakpakpa wɔ dalɩ amʋ́ oŋainkpa.
translated: Early in the morning, the rooster crows loudly at the door of the house.

ref: You protected me from violent enemies and made me much greater than all of them.
text: Mʋtɛlɛ mɩ lɛ mɩ alupʋ ɩbɩ. Fotsu mɩ fʋa dʋn amʋ́; fɛlɛ mɩ lɛ awuntɔlɩnpʋ ɩbɩ.
translated: My father is a farmer. Every day he goes to the farm; my mother is a trader.

ref: You give glorious victories to your chosen king. Your faithful love for David and for his descendants will never end.
text: Fʋtɛha fʋ owie ɩsʋgyi akpɔnkpɔntɩ. Fʋtɛlɛ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka suna mɩ Dawid ánɩ́ fada ofuli mʋa mʋ abi-aná brɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: Open your mouth wide. Open it so that I will put words in it like David who said that he would not keep quiet before his enemies.

ref: They don't speak a word, and there is never the sound of a voice.
text: Bʋmɛdɛ ɔblɩ kʋkʋ blɩ, bʋmɛdɛtɔɩ, tamɛ anɩdɛ mʋ asɩ nu.
translated: The yam is not yet cooked, the yam farmer, wait a little longer.

ref: The Lord's instruction is right; it makes our hearts glad. His commands shine brightly, and they give us light.
text: Nana Bulu mbla ɩda ɔkpa, ɩtɛha ansɩ tegyi ɔha. Mʋ mbla lɔwankɩ. Ɩtɛtɩtɩ ɔha ansɩsʋ.
translated: Nana Bulu has a farm, he goes there every day. He has a bicycle. He rides it every morning.

ref: Don't let me do wrong on purpose, Lord, or let sin have control over my life. Then I will be innocent, and not guilty of some terrible fault.
text: Máha mɩ fʋ osumbi anfɩ ankɩansɩ bwɛ lakpan. Máha mʋ́ igyi mɩsʋ, mɛnɩ ɔlala méetsia mɩ ɩwɩ. Fówun mméegyi ɩpɔn ánɩ́ nabwɛ lakpan kpɔnkpɔntɩ.
translated: Yesterday I went to the farm and saw a big snake. Yesterday I was afraid, but today I am not afraid. You should not be afraid when you see a big snake in the bush.

ref: You did what he wanted most and never told him “No.”
text: Tɔá ɔlɔkʋlɩ tsu mʋ ɩklʋntɔ faha mʋ a. Fʋmekina tɔá ɔlɛfɩtɛ fʋ. Ɩya.
translated: Continue reading this and answer the questions that follow. You have finished reading. Good.

ref: You, Lord, brought me safely through birth, and you protected me when I was a baby at my mother's breast.
text: Fʋlɛlɛ mɩ tsu mɩ yin opunitɔ. Fɛha nɔpʋ ansɩ dɩnka fʋsʋ brɛá ndenyɔpʋ.
translated: Yesterday I went to my friend's house. There we sat and talked until it became dark.

ref: Don't let lions eat me. You rescued me from the horns of wild bulls,
text: Lɛ mɩ tsu agyata anfɩ ɔnɔ. Tsú akpokpo anfɩ bʋbʋ ɩyɩn anfɩ nsiebi ɔnɔ.
translated: So I bought a goat for him. I also bought a fowl and some eggs for him.

ref: The Lord doesn't hate or despise the helpless in all of their troubles. When I cried out, he listened and did not turn away.
text: Tsufɛ ɔtamakɩ ohiani mʋ ipian owunkɛ, ki nwun. Ɔtamadamli ɔma ha mʋ. Osu kpoli mʋ a, ɔtɔsɔ mʋ ɔnɔ.
translated: The rich person does not give to the poor, and he dies. His riches are left behind. When the rainy season comes, the mushrooms grow.

ref: You treat me to a feast, while my enemies watch. You honor me as your guest, and you fill my cup until it overflows.
text: Fatswɩ ɔpʋnʋ mɩ alupʋ ansɩtɔ ha mɩ. Faklɩ mɩ aba. Mɩ onunwe labʋla, idetsitsa.
translated: He/she/they took the basket and put yams in it. The woman came. She greeted him/her/them, they responded.

ref: Who may climb the Lord's hill or stand in his holy temple?
text: Ma ɔbɔ́dʋ Nana Bulu ɩbʋ amʋ? Ma ɔbɛ́lɩɩ mʋ ɔtswɛkpa ɩnʋ?
translated: Did you see Nana Bulu today? Did you greet him this morning?

ref: I ask only one thing, Lord: Let me live in your house every day of my life to see how wonderful you are and to pray in your temple.
text: Toku kʋlɛ pɛ ndɛ Nana Bulu kʋlɩ. Mʋ́ ɔkpa négya. Mʋ́gyi; nétsia mʋ woyitɔ mɩ nkpatɔ fɛ́ɛ́, pʋ mɩ ansibi wun alɩá ɔbʋ alɛ, gywɩɩn mʋ́ ɩwɩ mʋ ɔtswɛkpa ɩnʋ.
translated: Long ago, Nana Bulu lived. He was a hunter. One day; as he was going to the forest to hunt animals, he met a big snake on the way, and it blocked his path.

ref: My heart tells me to pray. I am eager to see your face,
text: Mɩ ɩklʋn ɔbɛɛ, “Dunka mʋ!” Mʋ́ mbɛɛ, “Nana Bulu, fʋ ndedunka.”
translated: "I said, 'Sing for me!' He said, 'Grandfather Bulu, you sing first.'"

ref: Don't let them do to me what they want. People tell lies about me and make violent threats,
text: Mási mɩ, ankpa wie mɩ alupʋ ɩbɩtɔ, tsufɛ adansɩfɔ afunupʋ bakʋsʋ mɩsʋ, bʋdɛ mɩ huna.
translated: After that, when I finished eating, I went to the room, took my school bag, and left the house.

ref: All you angels in heaven, honor the glory and power of the Lord!
text: Ɔsʋsʋ atumipʋ, mlɩkanfʋ Nana Bulu. Mlɩpʋ numnyam mʋ́a ɔwʋnlɩn ha mʋ.
translated: The chief is powerful, his name is Nana Bulu. He is respected by everyone here.

ref: The voice of the Lord destroys the cedar trees; the Lord shatters cedars on Mount Lebanon.
text: Mʋ ɔmɛ tebiabia Lebanon ndum. Itebiabia Lebanon odum akpɔnkpɔntɩ blublublu.
translated: He/she has never visited Lebanon. Visiting Lebanon is very difficult.

ref: and the desert tremble. And because of the Lord, the desert near Kadesh shivers and shakes.
text: Mʋ ɔmɛ tɔpʋtɩ kpɩnkɩ adimbi ɔsʋlʋ. Ɩtɛkpɩnkɩ Kades adimbi ɔsʋlʋ ɛ.
translated: He bought a red shirt yesterday. The red shirt is Kade’s.

ref: Pray that our Lord will make us strong and give us peace.
text: Nana Bulu tɔwa mʋ ɔmá ɔwʋnlɩn, ɔtɔpʋ iwilwii yula amʋ́.
translated: Nana Bulu bought a goat and gave it to his friend, who was very happy.

ref: I felt secure and thought, “I'll never be shaken!”
text: Brɛá ɩlɔwa alɛ ha mɩ a, nɛblɩ mbɛɛ, “Tɔtɔ mɛ́ɛtalɩ mɩ.”
translated: "When the child saw me, he/she said, 'Give me my thing.'"

ref: You, Lord God, are my mighty rock and my fortress. Lead me and guide me, so that your name will be honored.
text: Fʋgyi mɩ ɩbʋta pʋ́ mɩ oŋainkpa kpikpli. Fʋ ɩda sʋ gya mɩ nkpa, afʋkpa mɩ.
translated: You should not eat food and talk at the same time. You must finish eating before you talk, my friend.

ref: You are faithful, and I trust you because you rescued me.
text: Napʋ mɩ ɔŋɛ wa fʋ ɩbɩtɔ. Oo! Nana Bulu, Bulu Ɔnɔkwalɩpʋ, fahɔ mɩ.
translated: Today I am here with you all. Oh! Nana Bulu, Bulu the Wise One, help me.

ref: The Lord destroys the plans and spoils the schemes of the nations.
text: Nana Bulu tɛha ɔmá-ɔmá asʋn tesin amʋ́ ɔnɔ, oteyinta amʋ́ nhɩhɩɛtɔ.
translated: Nana Bulu gives gifts to the children every year, especially during the festival.

ref: The Lord looks at the world
text: Otowun atɔ tsu ɔsʋsʋ kein, ɔdɛ anyankpʋsa fɛ́ɛ́ wun.
translated: If a person eats these leaves, he will become very strong.

ref: The Lord is there to rescue all who are discouraged and have given up hope.
text: Ɔma ɩfɔ aha ánɩ́ ɩlakpɔn amʋ́ wa. Ɔtɔhɔ aha ánɩ́ ɔkla lasrɩ amʋ́.
translated: He gives the man a goat. She buys the man a sheep.

ref: Shield me and help me.
text: Kʋsʋ tsu fʋ akɔtɔ, afʋtin amʋ́ ɔkpa!
translated: If you want fish, you must get your hands wet!

ref: I will celebrate and be joyful because you, Lord, have saved me.
text: Nana Bulu sʋ ansɩ bégyi mɩ. Ɩwɩ bɛ́sankɩ mɩ, tsufɛ alahɔ mɩ nkpa.
translated: Nana Bulu gave me a goat. I will take care of it, so that it will grow well.

ref: The wicked will lose all of their power, but the Lord gives strength to everyone who is good.
text: Nana Bulu tɛkpa lalahɛ ɔbwɛpʋ ɩba, tamɛ ɔtɔwa yile ɔbwɛpʋ ɔwʋnlɩn.
translated: Nana Bulu climbed the mountain yesterday, but he came down the mountain today.

ref: You, Lord, know every one of my deepest desires, and my noisy groans are no secret to you.
text: Mɩ Wie, fʋyin tɔá ndedunka. Tɔá sʋá ndesu wa ɩwɩtɔ ɩda fʋ ansɩtɔ.
translated: As for me, I am tired today. Today I will not go with you to the farm.

ref: I told you my sins, and I am sorry for them.
text: Nalɛ mɩ lakpan blɩ. Ɩlahɔ mɩ ɔsɩn ánɩ́ nabwɛ mʋ́.
translated: Yesterday I went to the farm. I saw a big snake there.

ref: I felt a fire burning inside, and the more I thought, the more it burned, until at last I said:
text: Mɩ klʋn lɛblɩ ogya wa mɩ ɩwɩtɔ pɩnpɩɩnpɩn. Brɛá ndɛ mʋ́ ɩwɩ gywɩɩn kotoo a, iletsii ogya. Mʋ́ sʋ nɛblɩ mbɛɛ,
translated: I put firewood on the fire and it burned brightly. When the food was cooked, I removed it from the fire. I did not add more firewood.

ref: “Listen, Lord, to my prayer! My eyes are flooded with tears, as I pray to you. I am merely a stranger visiting in your home as my ancestors did.
text: “Oo Nana Bulu, nu mɩ mpaɩ, afʋnu mɩ isu. Mákina nu. Tsufɛ ɔfɔɔ ngyi, ndɛka tsʋn kɛkɛ fɛ mɩ anain.
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, give me water, I am thirsty. I am begging you. If you do not give, I will die here in your presence.

ref: Our Lord, let your worshipers rejoice and be glad. They love you for saving them, so let them always say, “The Lord is wonderful!”
text: Tamɛ ha ɩwɩ ɩsankɩ aha ánɩ́ bʋtodunka fʋ fɛ́ɛ́, abʋnya fʋ ɩwɩ ansigyi. Ansɩ igyi aha ánɩ́ fʋ nkpahɔ legyi amʋ́ ansɩ, abʋkplʋn blɩ lablɩ bɛɛ, “Nana Bulu lomoni!”
translated: When you go to the farm and you see a python, do not be afraid. If you are not afraid and you greet it respectfully, the python will respond, “Nana Bulu greets you!”

ref: Then my enemies won't defeat me, and I will know that you really care.
text: Ɩlaha nawun ánɩ́ fʋtekle mɩ asʋn, tsufɛ fʋmɛ́ɛha mɩ alupʋ abʋnya mɩ ɩwɩ ansigyi.
translated: If you want to be wise, always listen to the advice of elders and follow their good example.

ref: You have helped me because I am innocent, and you will always be close to my side.
text: Ɔlala ɩma mɩ ɩwɩ, sʋ fatsu mɩ lɩɩ tɩntɩn. Faha ansɩ dɛ mɩgyi fʋ ansɩtɔ brɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: Grandfather gave me a goat, but the rope was very short. That is why I gave it to you so that you would tie it well.

ref: Why am I discouraged? Why am I restless? I trust you, Lord! And I will praise you again because you help me, and you are my God.
text: Mɩ ɔkla, ntogyi sʋ asʋn dɛ fʋ han alɩ? Ntogyi sʋ fawa nwɛ alɩ? Pʋ ansɩ dɩnka Bulusʋ, tsufɛ nɛ́lakanfʋ mɩ bʋalɛ ogyipʋ pʋ́ mɩ Bulu.
translated: My friend, why are you sad today? Why are you not happy? When I saw Paul, he told me that you and he had a quarrel.

ref: I run to you for protection. Why do you turn me away? Why must enemies mistreat me and make me sad?
text: Tsufɛ fʋgyi mɩ Bulu pʋ́ mɩ ɔtanʋkpa. “Ntogyi sʋ fasi mɩ wa ɔkpa? Ntogyi sʋ mɩ alupʋ bʋdɩn mɩsʋ, mʋ́ sʋ nɔ́wa nwɛ natɩ tsʋntsʋn?”
translated: Then the Dog said to the Hare, “Why do you always run away from me? Why don’t you come and play with me, so that we can be friends?”

ref: You sold your people for little or nothing, and you earned no profit.
text: Fafɛ fʋ aha ibia pɔnhɛ, fʋmenya tɔtɔ anɩsʋ.
translated: If you go early to the farm, you will get plenty of mushrooms.

ref: We face death all day for you. We are like sheep on their way to be slaughtered.
text: Ee! Fʋ sʋ anɩtefia lowu ɔpa-onye, fɛ akufa bapʋ bɛya ɔmɔkpa.
translated: Yes! If you see a chief coming, it means his subjects are coming with him.

ref: Mighty king, glorious ruler, strap on your sword
text: Otumipʋ, tsu fʋ ɔdayi tsan. Kɩta fʋ numnyam pʋ́ fʋ iwiegyitɔ.
translated: Almighty, give him/her a peaceful rest. Receive him/her into your glory.

ref: You love justice and hate evil. And so, your God chose you and made you happier than any of your friends.
text: Fʋtekle yilebwɛ. Lalahɛbwɛ mʋ́ a, fʋtekisi. Mʋ́ sʋ fʋ Wie Bulu lada fʋ ofuli, pʋ ansigyi bʋla fʋtɔ dʋn fʋ aba.
translated: The yam is rotten. If you see it, throw it away. If you do not, your mother will cook it for you to eat, and you will get sick and your stomach will hurt.

ref: God is our mighty fortress, always ready to help in times of trouble.
text: Bulu gyi anɩ okpikplikpa pʋ́ anɩ ɔwʋnlɩn. Ɔma ɔka anɩ wa anɩ ɔkplankɛ.
translated: The goat went to the garden and ate the vegetables. He called his friend and they both ate together.

ref: The Lord Most High is fearsome, the ruler of all the earth.
text: tsufɛ Nana Bulu, Ɔsʋsʋʋsʋ mʋ Wie amʋ ɩwɩ bʋ ifu. Owie kpɔnkpɔɔnkpɔntɩ ogyi ɔyɩ fɛ́ɛ́sʋ.
translated: Nana Bulu died, his children cried a lot. They cried loudly all day long.

ref: God is there to defend it and has proved to be its protector.
text: Bulu onuto bʋ awie amʋ woyitɔ. Alalɛ ɩwɩ ɔwan ánɩ́ mʋgyi ɔtanʋkpa kinkin ha mʋ́.
translated: The dog came and ate my food. Then I took a big stick and chased it away.

ref: Our God, here in your temple we think about your love.
text: Oo Bulu, anɩdɛ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka ɩwɩ gywɩɩn fʋ ɔtswɛkpa ɩnʋ.
translated: Mr. Bulu, today you should go and bring your younger sibling from the farm.

ref: Soon they will die and all their wealth will be left behind.
text: Tsufɛ nɩ owu a, omóotsu kʋkʋ natɩ. Mʋ numnyam ɛ ɩmɛ́ɛkpa mʋ ya.
translated: When night comes, the children sleep. I am tired so I will sleep too.

ref: God shines brightly from Zion, the most beautiful city.
text: Bulu tɔwankɩ tsu Sion, wuluá ɩbʋ akɩlɛ ɔyɩtɔ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: The rain falls on Zion, the people rejoice greatly.

ref: “Call my followers together! They offered me a sacrifice, and we made an agreement.”
text: Ɔbɛɛ, “Mlɩkpa aha ánɩ́ bʋtamakpa mɩ ɔma wa abatɔ ha mɩ. Aha amʋʋ bʋtɔbɔ afɔdɩɛ, ka ntam ha mɩ amʋ!”
translated: He said, “Give me water so I can wash my hands and eat with you. I am very hungry, give me food, and let me eat with you!”

ref: You made friends with every crook you met, and you liked people who break their wedding vows.
text: Fowun owikplu fakpla buo mʋ. Fʋa mbʋa atɔpʋ bʋ mba.
translated: The woman is pounding fufu in the room. She is using a wooden pestle to pound it.

ref: Turn your eyes from my sin and cover my guilt.
text: Bun ansibi mɩ lakpansʋ, afʋtsitsi mɩ lalahɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: If you do not work, you will not get food.

ref: Offerings and sacrifices are not what you want.
text: tsufɛ afɔdɩɛ tamagyi fʋ ansɩ. Nɩ nɔbɔ atɔtɔhɛ-afɔdɩɛ ha fʋ kʋraa a, fʋmedekle.
translated: If you do not perform the sacrifice well, you will not get anything. But if you perform the thanksgiving sacrifice well, you will get a blessing.

ref: You people may be strong and brag about your sins, but God can be trusted day after day.
text: Ɔha yinhɛ, ntogyi sʋ fʋdɛ ɩwɩ adabi da, lalahɛá fʋdɛpʋgyi Bulu aha sʋ? Ekekegyiɛkɛ ɔyɩkɛhɛ fɛ́ɛ́,
translated: In the past, why did people not eat dog meat, but now some people eat it? The answer is not known.

ref: You plan brutal crimes, and your lying words cut like a sharp razor.
text: fʋdɛ lalahɛ susu. Ofunupʋ olembiapʋ, fʋ ɔdandʋ bʋ ogya fɛ ɔdayibi.
translated: The child is crying. His mother is not here, so he is afraid of the darkness and the fire.

ref: you will punish my enemies for their evil deeds. Be my faithful friend and destroy them.
text: Pʋ mɩ alupʋ lalahɛ ka amʋ́ ɩkɔ. Fʋbʋ ɔnɔkwalɩ ha mɩ, sʋ mɔ amʋ́.
translated: If you want to go outside, tell me first. Don't just go without telling me.

ref: because my enemies attack with loud shouts. They treat me terribly and hold angry grudges.
text: mɩ alupʋ asʋn blɩhɛ pʋ́ aha lalahɛ asɩá bʋdɛ mɩ ba sʋ. Tsufɛ bapʋ ipian dɩnka mɩsʋ, mɩ ɩwɩ ɔblɔ laha bʋdɛ mɩ lu.
translated: When I was going to the farm yesterday, it started to rain before I arrived. Because of the heavy rain, I returned home, I did not go to the farm.

ref: But it was my closest friend, the one I trusted most.
text: Tamɛ fʋ mɩ ba, mɩ nyawie pʋ́ mɩ ɔbɩsʋfɛá nhɩɛ nyin fʋ, dɛpʋ ɩnɩ gyi mɩ.
translated: If you come to me, I will show you the teacher who taught me, so that you can ask him.

ref: Do you mighty people talk only to oppose justice? Don't you ever judge fairly?
text: Atumipʋ, lɛlɛ mlɩtɛblɩ asʋn ánɩ́ ɩda ɔkpa? Mlɩtegyi asʋn ɔnɔkwalɩsʋ ha aha?
translated: Chief, how many times have I told you this? Why do you not listen to me?

ref: You are always planning evil, and you are brutal.
text: O-o, lalahɛ bʋ mlɩ ɩklʋntɔ. Owuntɔlɩnla mlɩtɔbwɛ ɔsʋlʋsʋ.
translated: Yes, that is my friend. My friend is sitting under the tree.

ref: Your words spread poison like the bite of a cobra
text: Bʋbʋ elin fɛ ɩwɔ. Bʋgyi sapa ɔkʋá oletila asʋ.
translated: The goat is here. The sheep is eating grass outside.

ref: Those liars return at evening, growling like dogs roaming the city.
text: Bʋteyinki ba ntʋpwɛ, bʋdɛkplʋn fɛ akɩaɩ, bomli wulu amʋ.
translated: Children are playing, they are laughing together, everyone is happy.

ref: You made the earth shake and split wide open; now heal its wounds and stop its trembling.
text: Fʋlɛha ɔsʋlʋ lɛkpɩnkɩ, ɩlɛbaba. Yinkia mʋ́ tsa aba, tsufɛ idekle ɩmaɩn-maɩn.
translated: The hunter went to the forest, he is brave. When he saw an animal, he quickly took his gun.

ref: You are a strong tower, where I am safe from my enemies.
text: Tsufɛ fʋgyi mɩ ɔtanʋkpa, obu fʋahɛá iletri mɩ alupʋ sʋ.
translated: If you see a snake, do not throw stones or sticks at it.

ref: I feel like a shaky fence or a sagging wall. How long will all of you attack and assault me?
text: Ɔmɛnkɛ mlési mɩ nkʋlɛ han? Mlɩ fɛ́ɛ́ mlɩdekleá mlɛ́lɛ mɩ ɩnʋ, fɛ bunti ntɛ ɩban bwiehɛ?
translated: Where will you sleep today? I want to come and see you, or should I wait until tomorrow?

ref: Don't trust in violence or depend on dishonesty or rely on great wealth.
text: Mápʋ ansɩ dɩnka adufudɩɛ atɔsʋ. Másusuá fénya tɔkʋ tsu owitɔ. Nɩ fʋ atɔ ɩklɛɩ kʋraa a, mápʋ ansɩ dɩnka mʋ́sʋ.
translated: If you do not buy enough food, you will be hungry. Always make sure you have enough money. If you do not have any money at all, you will be hungry.

ref: Your love means more than life to me, and I praise you.
text: Fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka bʋ alɛ dʋn nkpa. Mʋ́ sʋ nɛ́kanfʋ fʋ.
translated: You should not say such things to your friend. I am advising you.

ref: I think about you, God, before I go to sleep, and my thoughts turn to you during the night.
text: Nɩ nda ɔklansʋ oo ntɛkaɩn fʋsʋ. Ntɔgywɩɩn fʋ ɩwɩ onye fɛ́ɛ́,
translated: If you go to the farm, be careful. There are snakes there, my friend.

ref: Everyone will come to you because you answer prayer.
text: Mpaɩ onupʋ, fʋ wa anyankpʋsa fɛ́ɛ́ bɛ́ba.
translated: Don’t worry, your friend will come soon.

ref: You take care of the earth and send rain to help the soil grow all kinds of crops. Your rivers never run dry, and you prepare the earth to produce abundant grain.
text: Fʋtɛbɛkɩ ɔsʋlʋ, wʋlɩ ntsu mʋ́sʋ, ha mʋ́ ɩtɔwa nfɔ. Fʋtɛha ntsu tɔbʋla fʋ abʋnkpa, ɩtɛha amʋ́ atɔ-abi tɔwa alɛ. Tsufɛ alɩ fɛhɩɛ mʋ́ nɩ.
translated: The football is new, but its color is not good, and it is not very big. The football's color is black and white, but its shape is round. Some people like it.

ref: But let your people be happy and celebrate because of you.
text: Tamɛ ha aha yile abʋgyi ɔdwɛ. Bʋfuli kpayi ansigyisʋ fʋ ansɩtɔ, hɩɛ nya ansigyi.
translated: Let us go to the farm tomorrow morning. Prepare your hoe and your cutlass, so we can go early.

ref: Your people settled there, and you were generous to everyone in need.
text: Fʋ aha bɔpʋ ɩnʋ bwɛ otsiakpa. Oo Bulu, fʋ awɩtɔlɛ sʋ fɔbʋla ahianfɔ.
translated: You should not eat this food. Oh Bulu, you must listen to your elders.

ref: Our Lord and our God, your terrible enemies are ready for war, but you will crush their skulls.
text: Tamɛ obésisri mʋ alupʋ nwun. Amʋ́ na bʋdɛ ɩpɔngyila bwɛ nɩ.
translated: He will cook yam for his mother. His mother is very happy about it.

ref: The God of Israel is fearsome in his temple, and he makes us strong. Let's praise our God!
text: Bulu ɩwɩ bʋ ifu mʋ otsiakpa ɩnʋ. Israel Bulu tɔpʋ mʋ tumi mʋ́a ɔwʋnlɩn ha amʋ́. Mlɩkanfʋ Bulu!
translated: God gave them a king. Israel's God has power to help and to make strong. Praise God!

ref: Lord God All-Powerful, ruler of Israel, don't let me embarrass anyone who trusts and worships you.
text: Oo anɩ Wie, Ɩsa Ɔnɔ Ɔlɩɩpʋ, Nana Bulu, mɩ sʋ máha pɛlɩ ɩkɩta fʋ ɔkpa akɩpʋ. Oo Israel Bulu, mɩ sʋ máha fʋ adunkapʋ abʋsʋpa.
translated: You are the Lord, the God Almighty, Father Bulu, I have come to ask you for your help today. You God of Israel, I have come to ask you for your protection and blessings.

ref: My love for your house burns in me like a fire, and when others insult you, they insulted me as well.
text: Fʋ otsiakpa ɩnʋ asʋn ɩhɩɛ bʋ mɩ ogya. Fʋ asiabi fɛ́ɛ́ lɛbɛdɩnka mɩsʋ.
translated: You should not put your hand in fire. Your fingers will get burned.

ref: But I pray to you, Lord. So when the time is right, answer me and help me with your wonderful love.
text: Tamɛ mɩ mʋ́ fʋ Nana Bulu netsia su kpoli. Oo Bulu, brɛá iletsia fʋ a, pʋ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka pʋ́ fʋ nkpahɔá igyi ɔnɔkwalɩ sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ.
translated: When I see Nana Bulu, I become happy. Oh Bulu, whenever you come, bring your wisdom so that your words will give us knowledge and guide us.

ref: They are like trees growing beside a stream, trees that produce fruit in season and always have leaves. Those people succeed in everything they do.
text: Ogyi fɛ oyiá boswie mʋ́ ntsu ɔnɔ, itoswie abi mʋ́ swiebɩ, ɩtamakpa atɛ. Togyitɔá ɔbwɛ tɔwa alɛ ha mʋ.
translated: He/she/they harvest yams in the ninth month, maize in the sixth month, and groundnuts in the fifth month. The harvest is usually abundant at that time.

ref: The Lord protects everyone who follows him, but the wicked follow a road that leads to ruin.
text: Tsufɛ Nana Bulu tɛkpa yile abwɛpʋ, tamɛ lalahɛ abwɛpʋ mʋ́ bɛ́ta.
translated: When Nana Bulu enters the room, everyone in the room stands up.

ref: The Lord becomes furious and threatens them. His anger terrifies them as he says,
text: Mʋ́ ɔma a, ɔbɛ́bla amʋ́ asʋn ɔblɔsʋ, wa ɩyɩn wa amʋ́ ɔbɛɛ,
translated: If he comes, he will take the water from the pot, and give it to his mother.

ref: because you protect and bless your people.
text: Nana Bulu, fʋ ɩbɩtɔ nkpahɔ bʋ. Ha fʋ oyula ɩba fʋ abisʋ. Ɩya.
translated: Nana Bulu, come and sit here. Bring your chair and sit. Okay.

ref: How long will you people refuse to respect me? You love foolish things, and you run after what is worthless.
text: Oo anyankpʋsa, mlɩméesi mɩ kpɔɩ? Mlɩméesi asʋn ánɩ́ ɩma labi ɔma gya, si afunu buo? Ɩya.
translated: My friend, have you eaten? I ate yam with palm oil, and what did you eat? Yes.

ref: Offer the proper sacrifices and trust the Lord.
text: Mlɩbɔ afɔdɩɛ wanklaan, amlɩpʋ ansɩ dɩnka Nana Bulusʋ.
translated: When the chief was absent, the elders did not mention Nana Bulusu's name.

ref: You brought me more happiness than a rich harvest of grain and grapes.
text: Faha ansɩ lahɩɛ gyi mɩ, dʋn ansɩá itegyi amʋ́ brɛá amʋ́ atogyihɛ pʋ́ amʋ́ nta labusʋ.
translated: If you do not listen to me, you will not know what I am teaching and you will not pass the exam.

ref: Let all who run to you for protection always sing joyful songs. Provide shelter for those who truly love you and let them rejoice.
text: Tamɛ ha aha ánɩ́ bʋtɛsrɩ tanʋ fʋ fɛ́ɛ́ abʋnya ansigyi. Ha amʋ́ abʋwa ansigyi ɩlʋ. Bun amʋ́sʋ, mɛnɩ ansɩ bégyi fʋ adwɛpʋ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: If you want to get something, you must work hard. If you do not work hard, you will not get what you want. So, my child, always work hard.

ref: Don't punish me, Lord, or even correct me when you are angry!
text: Oo Nana Bulu, máwa ɩyɩn wa mɩ ɔblɔsʋ. Mákʋla ɔblɔ bɩtɩ mɩ ɩsʋ!
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, give me your blessing. I need your blessing today!

ref: and I am in deep distress. How long will it be?
text: Oo Nana Bulu, ifu dɛ mɩ ɔkla. Máha mʋ́ ɩwa ɔpá asa afʋtsa mɩ ɩlɔ.
translated: Yes Nana Bulu, you are right. I will give you some of the food I cooked.

ref: Or else they will rip me apart like lions attacking a victim, and no one will save me.
text: Nɩ megyi alɩ bɛ́kɩta mɩ, tɩtɩ mɩ fɛ gyata; mma ɔhɔpʋ.
translated: I will go but if I meet a lion; I will not continue.

ref: You, God, are my shield, the protector of everyone whose heart is right.
text: Bulu legyi ɩban wa mɩ. Mʋtɔhɔ aha ánɩ́ amʋ́ asʋn da ɔkpa.
translated: The dog came to my house. The woman said that she will go to the farm tomorrow.

ref: Whenever your enemies refuse to change their ways, you sharpen your sword and string your bow.
text: Nɩ bʋmɛtsɛ amʋ́ itsiatɔ a, ɔbɛ́ya mʋ ɔdayi. Alabɩtɩ mʋ ɔbɩn dodo.
translated: If you ask him for money, he will give it to you. But he does not have much.

ref: The trouble they cause comes back on them, and their heads are crushed by their own evil deeds.
text: Amʋ́ onuto lalahɛ teyinki ba amʋ́sʋ. Amʋ́ owuntɔlɩn teyinki bobun amʋ́ nwunsʋ.
translated: He/she/they did not eat the food because he/she/they were not hungry. He/she/they did not drink water because he/she/they were not thirsty.

ref: Our Lord and Ruler, your name is wonderful everywhere on earth! You let your glory be seen in the heavens above.
text: Oo anɩ Wie Nana Bulu, fʋ ɩda lɔkʋkʋ moni ɔyɩtɔ o! Fʋ numnyam lɔfʋa dafa nwʋlʋta.
translated: You are like Nana Bulu, you always want to take other people's money! Your greed will bring you trouble.

ref: the birds in the sky, the fish in the sea, and all ocean creatures.
text: mbubwi mʋ́a ntsutso-aye pʋ́ ntobiá bʋna ɔpʋtɔ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: The old woman is sitting under the tree with her child.

ref: Our Lord and Ruler, your name is wonderful everywhere on earth!
text: Oo anɩ Wie Nana Bulu, fʋ ɩda lɔkʋkʋ moni ɔyɩtɔ o!
translated: You are like Nana Bulu, you never run out of money!

ref: You judge the world fairly and treat all nations with justice.
text: Obégyi ɔyɩtɔ aha fɛ́ɛ́ asʋn mʋ́ ɔkpasʋ. Obégyi ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́sʋ ɔnɔkwalɩsʋ.
translated: He/she will take water to the farm with a calabash. He/she will take food to the teacher.

ref: You did not forget to punish the guilty or listen to the cries of those in need.
text: Tsufɛ mʋtɛka ɔha ánɩ́ alawa ɩbɩ obugyatɔ ɩkɔ. Ɔtamakina ipian awunpʋ isu nu.
translated: He bought a car but he has not paid the money. The machine is still at the seller’s house.

ref: Please have mercy, Lord! My enemies mistreat me. Keep me from the gates that lead to death,
text: Oo Nana Bulu, wun mɩ nwɛ! Kɩ alɩá mɩ alupʋ bʋdɛ mɩ ipiantɔ wa! Fʋtɛlɛ mɩ tsu lowu ɔbɔnɔ,
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, listen to me! I have something important to tell you! Help me in this difficult situation,

ref: and I will sing about you at the gate to Zion. I will be happy there because you rescued me.
text: mɛnɩ nɛ́kanfʋ fʋ Sionfɔ ofiakpa. Ansɩ bégyi mɩ, tsufɛ fahɔ mɩ nkpa.
translated: If you forget me, O Sion, may my right hand wither. Let my tongue stick to my palate if I do not remember you.

ref: Do something, Lord! Don't let the nations win. Make them stand trial in your court of law.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, kʋsʋ! Máha nyankpʋsa ɔwa ɔkpayi fʋ ansɩtɔ ɔbɛɛ, magyi ɩsʋ. Ɔmá-ɔmá bʋgyi ɩpɔn fʋ ansɩtɔ!
translated: Yes, Nana Bulu, come! Bring your chair here and sit down, I am calling you. Let us talk together here and sit down!

ref: The wicked brag about their deepest desires. Those greedy people hate and curse you, Lord.
text: Tsufɛ aha lalahɛ bʋtɔpʋ amʋ́ ɔwɔlɩ lalahɛ tsu ɩwɩ. Bʋtoyula aha asisipʋ, kpɔɩ Nana Bulu.
translated: The chief sat under the tree and the people sat around him. The elders were there too, including Nana Bulu.

ref: The wicked are too proud to turn to you or even think about you.
text: Ɩwɩtsu sʋ bʋtamapʋ Bulu bwɛ tɔtɔ. Amʋ́ agywɩɩn fɛ́ɛ́ gyi, “Bulu ɔkʋkʋ má ɩnʋ.”
translated: Bwɛ’s child took Bulu’s basket. The mother shouted and said, “Bulu, your basket is gone.”

ref: Do something, Lord God, and use your powerful arm to help those in need.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, wa ɔsa bɩtɩ aha lalahɛ ɩsʋ, afʋkaɩn ɩwɩɔsɩn awunpʋsʋ.
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, when you go to the farm tomorrow, remember to bring your machete.

ref: But you see the trouble and the distress, and you will do something. The poor can count on you, and so can orphans.
text: Tamɛ togyitɔ ɩda fʋ ansɩtɔ, tsufɛ fʋdɛ ɩwɩɔsɩn pʋ́ ipian awunpʋ wun. Fʋtekleá fɔ́bʋa amʋ́ bregyibrɛ. Apɔnpʋ bʋtɔpʋ ansɩ dɩnka fʋsʋ, fʋtegyi awumbi bʋalɛ bregyibrɛ.
translated: If you want to be successful in life, you must work hard and be determined. Laziness leads to poverty, but hard work brings success.

ref: You listen to the longings of those who suffer. You offer them hope, and you pay attention to their cries for help.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, fʋtonu ɩwɩasɩ abapʋ isu. Fʋtɔwa amʋ́ ɔwʋnlɩn, yaa asʋ nu amʋ́ asʋn.
translated: Grandpa Bulu, your goat has eaten my yam seedlings. Your goat is always troublesome, and it always destroys my things.

ref: Everyone tells lies, and no one is sincere.
text: Ɔhagyiɔha dɛ afunu wa suna mʋ ba. Bʋbʋ ɔnɔwankɩ. Amʋ́ wʋlɛwʋlɛ bogyoli nfɩ, batsula nana.
translated: The old woman is sleeping in the room. Do not disturb her. She worked hard today, my child.

ref: From heaven the Lord looks down to see if anyone is wise enough to search for him.
text: Nana Bulu tɛkɩ anyankpʋsa fɛ́ɛ́ sɩsɩ tsu ɔsʋsʋ, kɩ bɛɛ, menya ɔtɩansɩpʋ ɔkʋá otodunka mʋ ɔkpa.
translated: Nana Bulu took the children to the farm early in the morning, and there, they found a big snake lying on the path.

ref: But all of them are corrupt; no one does right.
text: Amʋ́ fɛ́ɛ́ badamli ɔma ha Bulu. Amʋ́ fɛ́ɛ́ ɔkpa motswii. Yile ɔbwɛpʋ ɔkʋkʋ má ɩnʋ. Ɔbakʋlɛ kʋraa mɛ́dalɩ amʋ́tɔ.
translated: He gave Bulu some rice. He also gave him meat. Then he took water and drank. After that, he went to his farm.

ref: I long for someone from Zion to come and save Israel! Our Lord, when you bless your people again, Jacob's family will be glad, and Israel will celebrate.
text: Nhɩɛ ndɛ Nana Bulu kokoli mbɛɛ, ɔkʋ otsu Sionbʋsʋ bɔhɔ Israelfɔ. Ɛkɛá Bulu ɔbɛ́latsu mʋ aha kʋsʋ a, ɩhɩɛ wa Yakob abi-aná ɔdwɛ, ansɩ ɩhɩɛ gyi Israelfɔ.
translated: Listen now to what Nana Bulu says, he who gathers Sionbusu for Israel. This is what Bulu the Redeemer says, the Holy One of Yakob, to the one who is despised, to the one rejected by Israel.

ref: You, Lord, are all I want! You are my choice, and you keep me safe.
text: Nana Bulu, fʋgyi mɩ ogyikpa pʋ́ togyitɔá ɩbɔ́fʋn mɩ. Fʋtogyo mɩ ɔsʋlʋ ha mɩ.
translated: Nana Bulu, my father is a very good hunter. His gun is also very strong.

ref: I will always look to you, as you stand beside me and protect me from fear.
text: Mɩ ansɩ dɩn Nana Bulusʋ bregyibrɛ. Ɔlɩɩ mɩ gyɔpɩsʋ. Mʋ́ sʋ tɔtɔ ɩmɛ́ɛlɛ mɩ da.
translated: I'm telling you about Nana Bulusu's suffering. He is my grandfather. He took care of me when I was a child.

ref: I am your chosen one. You won't leave me in the grave or let my body decay.
text: tsufɛ fʋméesi mɩ wa afulitɔ. Mmɔkʋkpa fʋ ɔma. Mʋ́ sʋ fʋmɛ́ɛha mmɔ́ɔplɔ.
translated: The bird is sitting on the tree. The monkey is up. I saw the bird and the monkey.

ref: I am innocent, Lord! Won't you listen as I pray and beg for help? I am honest! Please hear my prayer.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, nu asʋn ánɩ́ ɩda ɔkpa, afʋnu mɩ isu! Nu mɩ mpaɩ, imotsu ofunupʋ ɔnɔ.
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, if you have heard my voice, help me! If you don't help me, my enemies will kill me.

ref: I don't do like others. I obey your teachings and am not cruel.
text: Fɛ alɩá akʋ bʋtɔbwɛ a, mɩ mʋ́ ndɛ fʋ mblatɔ. Ntamabwɛ awuntɔlɩnpʋ bwɛhɛ.
translated: If you finish the work, I will give you food. The children are playing outside.

ref: I have followed you, without ever stumbling.
text: Nna fʋ ɔkpasʋ bregyibrɛ. Mmɔkʋsrɛɩ dalɩ ɔkpa amʋsʋ.
translated: My father is very hardworking. Every morning he goes to the farm.

ref: Do something, Lord! Attack and defeat them. Take your sword and save me from those evil people.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, kʋsʋ lɩɩ mɩ alupʋsʋ, afʋba amʋ́ asɩ. Pʋ fʋ ɔdayi kɔ hɔ mɩ, tsu aha lalahɛ anfɩ ɩbɩtɔ.
translated: Grandfather Bulu said, "I am tired, let me rest a little." He did not go with them, but stayed at home and slept.

ref: You are good to me, Lord, because I do right, and you reward me because I am innocent.
text: Mɩ yilebwɛ sʋ Nana Bulu legyi mɩ bʋalɛ. Ɔlala ɩma mɩ ɩwɩ. Mʋ́ sʋ ɔlɔpʋ yile ka mɩ ɩkɔ.
translated: I greeted Nana Bulu before I left. He gave me money. He said I should buy something when I go.

ref: You rescue the humble, but you put down all who are proud.
text: Tsufɛ fʋtɔhɔ ɩwɩasɩ abapʋ nkpa, tamɛ fʋtɛba ɩwɩ atsupʋ asɩ.
translated: If you see a snake and run away, you will see its tail.

ref: I stuck my sword through my enemies, and they were crushed under my feet.
text: Nobwie amʋ́ bun mɩ ayabiasɩ, bʋmɛtalɩ tra kʋsʋ.
translated: If you want to marry a woman, you must pay a bride price.

ref: You made my enemies run, and I killed them.
text: Fɛha mɩ alupʋ bɛsanki amʋ́ yama srɩ. Nɔmɔ aha ánɩ́ bʋdɛ mɩ ɩsa kɔ.
translated: Yesterday my friend came to my house. We talked and then he went home.

ref: They cried out for help, but no one saved them; they called out to you, but there was no answer.
text: Bosu kpoli, tamɛ ɔhɔpʋ ɔkʋkʋ má ɩnʋ. Bosu kpoli Nana Bulu ɛ, tamɛ ɔmɛkpla amʋ́.
translated: Bosu kpoli, when he is hungry, he eats. Bosu kpoli Nana Bulu, when he is full, he sleeps.

ref: They have lost all courage, and from their fortresses, they come trembling.
text: Atɛtɔ lowu amʋ́, bʋdɛkpɩnkɩ kpakpakpakpa wɔ dalɩ amʋ́ oŋainkpa.
translated: The old man is walking, his sandals are making a clapping sound on the ground as he walks.

ref: You protected me from violent enemies and made me much greater than all of them.
text: Mʋtɛlɛ mɩ lɛ mɩ alupʋ ɩbɩ. Fotsu mɩ fʋa dʋn amʋ́; fɛlɛ mɩ lɛ awuntɔlɩnpʋ ɩbɩ.
translated: My father is a farmer. Every day he goes to the farm; my mother is a trader.

ref: I will praise you, Lord, and I will honor you among the nations.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, ɩnɩ sʋ nɛ́kanfʋ fʋ ɔmá-ɔmá ansɩtɔ. Nɔ́wa ɩlʋ kanfʋ fʋ ɩda.
translated: Yes, Nana Bulu, I have told you this story many times. Now I will tell you the story again today.

ref: You give glorious victories to your chosen king. Your faithful love for David and for his descendants will never end.
text: Fʋtɛha fʋ owie ɩsʋgyi akpɔnkpɔntɩ. Fʋtɛlɛ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka suna mɩ Dawid ánɩ́ fada ofuli mʋa mʋ abi-aná brɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: First you should listen carefully. Second you should remember that this is what David said to his father when he was going to his brothers in the field.

ref: They don't speak a word, and there is never the sound of a voice.
text: Bʋmɛdɛ ɔblɩ kʋkʋ blɩ, bʋmɛdɛtɔɩ, tamɛ anɩdɛ mʋ asɩ nu.
translated: If you eat a lot of food, you will get tired, and you will not be able to work.

ref: The Lord's instruction is right; it makes our hearts glad. His commands shine brightly, and they give us light.
text: Nana Bulu mbla ɩda ɔkpa, ɩtɛha ansɩ tegyi ɔha. Mʋ mbla lɔwankɩ. Ɩtɛtɩtɩ ɔha ansɩsʋ.
translated: Nana Bulu had a farm, he went there early in the morning. He worked hard. He returned home in the evening.

ref: Worshiping the Lord is sacred; he will always be worshiped. All his decisions are correct and fair.
text: Nana Bulu ifunya bʋ alɛ. Ɩbʋ ɩnʋ brɛ fɛ́ɛ́. Mʋ asʋnbua igyi ɔnɔkwalɩ. Mʋ́ fɛ́ɛ́ da ɔkpa.
translated: Nana Bulu has a goat. The goat is very big. The children are looking after it. They give it food every day.

ref: Don't let me do wrong on purpose, Lord, or let sin have control over my life. Then I will be innocent, and not guilty of some terrible fault.
text: Máha mɩ fʋ osumbi anfɩ ankɩansɩ bwɛ lakpan. Máha mʋ́ igyi mɩsʋ, mɛnɩ ɔlala méetsia mɩ ɩwɩ. Fówun mméegyi ɩpɔn ánɩ́ nabwɛ lakpan kpɔnkpɔntɩ.
translated: Yesterday I went to the farm and worked hard. Yesterday was very hot, so I was very tired. Today I am resting at home because of the hard work yesterday.

ref: You did what he wanted most and never told him “No.”
text: Tɔá ɔlɔkʋlɩ tsu mʋ ɩklʋntɔ faha mʋ a. Fʋmekina tɔá ɔlɛfɩtɛ fʋ. Ɩya.
translated: Continue the work so that we finish it today. Then come and eat. Okay.

ref: He asked to live a long time, and you promised him life that never ends.
text: Ɔlɔkʋlɩ nkpa, fɛha mʋ nkpa tɩntɩn; obétsia brɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: The elder is wise, his words are wise and deep; listen to him well.

ref: They will be destroyed by fire once you are here, and because of your anger, flames will swallow them.
text: Nɩ fɛba a, fɔ́pʋ obuntɔ ogya hɔ amʋ́. Fɔ́pʋ ɔblɔ mɛ amʋ́, ogya bɔ́hɔ amʋ́.
translated: If you go there, you will see fire there. If you don't see me there, the fire will burn there.

ref: and say, “Trust the Lord! If you are his favorite, let him protect you and keep you safe.”
text: “Mʋ ansɩ dɩn Nana Bulusʋ, ɔhɔ mʋ. Mʋ ansɩ tegyi mʋ ɩwɩ. Mʋ́ sʋ ɔlɛ mʋ!”
translated: "I am called Nana Bulusu, that is me. I am a chief here. I am the owner!"

ref: You, Lord, brought me safely through birth, and you protected me when I was a baby at my mother's breast.
text: Fʋlɛlɛ mɩ tsu mɩ yin opunitɔ. Fɛha nɔpʋ ansɩ dɩnka fʋsʋ brɛá ndenyɔpʋ.
translated: Yesterday I went to my friend's house. There we ate and drank together happily.

ref: From the day I was born, I have been in your care, and from the time of my birth, you have been my God.
text: Besi mɩ dɩnka fʋsʋ tsu brɛá bɔkwɩɩ mɩ. Tsu mɩ yin iputɔ ɔpá fʋgyi mɩ Bulu.
translated: Yesterday I went to the farm and worked hard. I returned home in the evening very tired.

ref: Enemies are all around like a herd of wild bulls. Powerful bulls from Bashan are everywhere.
text: Mɩ alupʋ bakafia mɩ, fɛ nnantswie yinhɛ tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ. Basan ɔmátɔ nnantswieá bʋbʋ ifu babomli mɩ.
translated: When I went to the farm, I saw many mushrooms. Some of them were growing under big trees.

ref: My enemies are like lions roaring and attacking with jaws open wide.
text: Mɩ alupʋ anfɩ bayaa ɔnɔ gblaun wa mɩ. Fɛ gyata ɔtrapʋ dekpoun, ɔdɛ ɔbwɩá alakɩta tɩtɩ.
translated: When I was going to the farm, I saw a leopard on the way. Like a lion hunts an antelope, it was chasing a goat quickly.

ref: Brutal enemies attack me like a pack of dogs, tearing at my hands and my feet.
text: Akɩaɩ bakafia mɩ. Aha lalahɛ bada ɔbʋn wa mɩ. Amʋ́ ayín lafuti mɩ ɩbɩ pʋ́ mɩ ayabi fɛ gyata.
translated: My father is strong. His voice is like a lion’s. I am not afraid because he protects me from the lion.

ref: Don't let lions eat me. You rescued me from the horns of wild bulls,
text: Lɛ mɩ tsu agyata anfɩ ɔnɔ. Tsú akpokpo anfɩ bʋbʋ ɩyɩn anfɩ nsiebi ɔnɔ.
translated: So I bought a goat for him. I bought a sheep and a fowl for his mother.

ref: The Lord doesn't hate or despise the helpless in all of their troubles. When I cried out, he listened and did not turn away.
text: Tsufɛ ɔtamakɩ ohiani mʋ ipian owunkɛ, ki nwun. Ɔtamadamli ɔma ha mʋ. Osu kpoli mʋ a, ɔtɔsɔ mʋ ɔnɔ.
translated: The rich man gives food to the poor, and they eat. The rich man is happy. When night comes, he sleeps well.

ref: You treat me to a feast, while my enemies watch. You honor me as your guest, and you fill my cup until it overflows.
text: Fatswɩ ɔpʋnʋ mɩ alupʋ ansɩtɔ ha mɩ. Faklɩ mɩ aba. Mɩ onunwe labʋla, idetsitsa.
translated: He/she/they took the basket and put the yams in it. The woman came. She greeted her, they talked.

ref: Who may climb the Lord's hill or stand in his holy temple?
text: Ma ɔbɔ́dʋ Nana Bulu ɩbʋ amʋ? Ma ɔbɛ́lɩɩ mʋ ɔtswɛkpa ɩnʋ?
translated: Did you see Nana Bulu today? Did you greet him this morning?

ref: I always look to you, because you rescue me from every trap.
text: Mɩ ansɩ dɩn Nana Bulusʋ brɛ fɛ́ɛ́, tsufɛ mʋ ɔbɛ́lɛ mɩ ayabi lɛ ayibitɔ.
translated: When I called Nana Buluso yesterday, he told me that he was not at home.

ref: I am lonely and troubled. Show that you care and have pity on me.
text: Damli ansɩ kɩ mɩ, afʋwun mɩ nwɛ. Tsufɛ ɔbakʋlɛ ngyi. Mɩ ɩla bʋ nwɛ.
translated: If you give me food, I will be happy. Today I am very hungry. I want to eat.

ref: I never forget your kindness, and I am always faithful to you.
text: Tsufɛ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka igya mɩ nkpa, nna fʋ ɔnɔkwalɩsʋ.
translated: If you light a lamp and put it in the room, then you are wise.

ref: I ask only one thing, Lord: Let me live in your house every day of my life to see how wonderful you are and to pray in your temple.
text: Toku kʋlɛ pɛ ndɛ Nana Bulu kʋlɩ. Mʋ́ ɔkpa négya. Mʋ́gyi; nétsia mʋ woyitɔ mɩ nkpatɔ fɛ́ɛ́, pʋ mɩ ansibi wun alɩá ɔbʋ alɛ, gywɩɩn mʋ́ ɩwɩ mʋ ɔtswɛkpa ɩnʋ.
translated: Long ago, there lived a chief called Nana Bulu. He was a brave man. They say; one day he went to the farm, and when he was returning home, he met a lion on the path, but he was not afraid and faced it.

ref: My heart tells me to pray. I am eager to see your face,
text: Mɩ ɩklʋn ɔbɛɛ, “Dunka mʋ!” Mʋ́ mbɛɛ, “Nana Bulu, fʋ ndedunka.”
translated: "I greeted him, 'Wake up!' He replied, 'Grandfather Bulu, you wake up.'"

ref: Don't let them do to me what they want. People tell lies about me and make violent threats,
text: Mási mɩ, ankpa wie mɩ alupʋ ɩbɩtɔ, tsufɛ adansɩfɔ afunupʋ bakʋsʋ mɩsʋ, bʋdɛ mɩ huna.
translated: This morning, when I was going to the farm, I saw three white birds on the road.

ref: All you angels in heaven, honor the glory and power of the Lord!
text: Ɔsʋsʋ atumipʋ, mlɩkanfʋ Nana Bulu. Mlɩpʋ numnyam mʋ́a ɔwʋnlɩn ha mʋ.
translated: The chief is powerful, his name is Nana Bulu. I have great respect for him in my heart.

ref: The voice of the Lord destroys the cedar trees; the Lord shatters cedars on Mount Lebanon.
text: Mʋ ɔmɛ tebiabia Lebanon ndum. Itebiabia Lebanon odum akpɔnkpɔntɩ blublublu.
translated: He/she has never visited Lebanon. Those who have visited Lebanon say it is very beautiful.

ref: and the desert tremble. And because of the Lord, the desert near Kadesh shivers and shakes.
text: Mʋ ɔmɛ tɔpʋtɩ kpɩnkɩ adimbi ɔsʋlʋ. Ɩtɛkpɩnkɩ Kades adimbi ɔsʋlʋ ɛ.
translated: He bought a red shirt yesterday. The red shirt is from Kade.

ref: Pray that our Lord will make us strong and give us peace.
text: Nana Bulu tɔwa mʋ ɔmá ɔwʋnlɩn, ɔtɔpʋ iwilwii yula amʋ́.
translated: Nana Bulu bought a goat for his child, he will give it to him tomorrow.

ref: I felt secure and thought, “I'll never be shaken!”
text: Brɛá ɩlɔwa alɛ ha mɩ a, nɛblɩ mbɛɛ, “Tɔtɔ mɛ́ɛtalɩ mɩ.”
translated: When the woman saw me, she said, “Come and sit with me.”

ref: You, Lord God, are my mighty rock and my fortress. Lead me and guide me, so that your name will be honored.
text: Fʋgyi mɩ ɩbʋta pʋ́ mɩ oŋainkpa kpikpli. Fʋ ɩda sʋ gya mɩ nkpa, afʋkpa mɩ.
translated: You should not talk too much. You must listen to me, my child.

ref: Protect me from hidden traps and keep me safe.
text: Lɛ mɩ lɛ yibiá bafɩnɩ mɩtɔ, tsufɛ fʋgyi mɩ ɔtanʋkpa.
translated: If I see a child there, I will give him/her a piece of bread.

ref: You are faithful, and I trust you because you rescued me.
text: Napʋ mɩ ɔŋɛ wa fʋ ɩbɩtɔ. Oo! Nana Bulu, Bulu Ɔnɔkwalɩpʋ, fahɔ mɩ.
translated: Today I am coming to your house. Oh! Nana Bulu, Bulu the Wise One, help me.

ref: I hate the worshipers of worthless idols, but I trust you, Lord.
text: Ntekisi ɩkpɩ atswɛpʋ. Mɩ mʋ́ ntɔpʋ ansɩ dɩnka fʋ Nana Bulusʋ.
translated: The teacher is at the farm. I have not gone there because of Nana Bulusʋ.

ref: I have known only sorrow all my life long, and I suffer year after year. I am weak from sin, and my bones are limp.
text: Isu lagyi mɩ nkpasʋ. Nawa nwɛ mɩ nkpatɔ fɛ́ɛ́. Mɩ lakpan sʋ ɔŋɛ lata mɩ, awútɔ labwiebwie mɩ.
translated: You are my friend. This is why I like you very much. You always help me when I am in trouble, and you always advise me.

ref: I will praise you, Lord, for showing great kindness when I was like a city under attack.
text: Ɩkanfʋ ɩbwɛ Nana Bulu klɛ, tsufɛ ɔlɛhɩɛ lɛ mʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka suna mɩ brɛá adʋn bɛkafia mɩ wulu, mma tɔtɔ bwɛtɔ.
translated: Long ago, when Nana Bulu lived, it is said that he used to go to the farm early in the morning before the sun rose, and he would return home late in the evening.

ref: All who trust the Lord, be cheerful and strong.
text: Mlɩ aha ánɩ́ mlɩdɛ Nana Bulu ɔkpa kɩ fɛ́ɛ́, mlɩwa ɩwɩ ɔwʋnlɩn, mlɩtomi, amlɩwa ɩklʋn.
translated: When I went to Nana Bulu’s house yesterday, I saw his children, his wife, and his dog.

ref: You bless them by saying, “You told me your sins, without trying to hide them, and now I forgive you.”
text: Ansɩ igyi ɔha ánɩ́ Nana Bulu mɔ́hɔ mʋ lalahɛ, afunu ɩma mʋtɔ.
translated: When they arrived at the place where Nana Bulu was, they found him dead.

ref: The Lord destroys the plans and spoils the schemes of the nations.
text: Nana Bulu tɛha ɔmá-ɔmá asʋn tesin amʋ́ ɔnɔ, oteyinta amʋ́ nhɩhɩɛtɔ.
translated: Nana Bulu gave gifts to his children, advising them to live in unity.

ref: The Lord looks at the world
text: Otowun atɔ tsu ɔsʋsʋ kein, ɔdɛ anyankpʋsa fɛ́ɛ́ wun.
translated: When the rain falls on the ground, it brings blessings to everyone.

ref: He protects them from death and starvation.
text: Ɔbɛ́lɛ amʋ́ tsu lowutɔ, hɔ amʋ́ nkpa akʋnbɩ.
translated: He/she will go to the farm tomorrow, and he/she will bring yam.

ref: I asked the Lord for help, and he saved me from all my fears.
text: Nosu kpoli Nana Bulu, ɔlɔsɔ mɩ ɔnɔ. Ɔlɛlɛ mɩ tsu ifu kugyikʋtɔ.
translated: We greeted Nana Bulu, he was at home. He gave us some roasted yam.

ref: The Lord is there to rescue all who are discouraged and have given up hope.
text: Ɔma ɩfɔ aha ánɩ́ ɩlakpɔn amʋ́ wa. Ɔtɔhɔ aha ánɩ́ ɔkla lasrɩ amʋ́.
translated: He/she gives you water when you are thirsty. He/she gives you food when you are hungry.

ref: Shield me and help me.
text: Kʋsʋ tsu fʋ akɔtɔ, afʋtin amʋ́ ɔkpa!
translated: If you want to buy a crab, don't fear its claws!

ref: Surprise them with disaster! Trap them in their own nets and let them fall and rot in the pits they have dug.
text: Ɩkplan ɩtʋ amʋ́, abʋkpa wie amʋ́ onuto ɔbɔ kwihɛ amʋtɔ, abʋda yibi amʋtɔ.
translated: If you do good, good will follow you, if you do bad, bad will follow you.

ref: I will celebrate and be joyful because you, Lord, have saved me.
text: Nana Bulu sʋ ansɩ bégyi mɩ. Ɩwɩ bɛ́sankɩ mɩ, tsufɛ alahɔ mɩ nkpa.
translated: Nana Bulu did not come with me. If he had come with me, I would not have been alone.

ref: They repay evil for good, and I feel all alone.
text: Bapʋ lalahɛ ka mɩ yile ɩkɔ, baha nawa nwɛ.
translated: They went to the farm and when they returned, they were tired.

ref: And when your people meet, I will praise you and thank you, Lord, in front of them all.
text: Fówun nɛ́da fʋ ɩpan fʋ aha tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ ansɩtɔ, kanfʋ fʋ aha tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ ansɩtɔ anɩ ɔpasuatɔ.
translated: If you do not put your hand into the gourd, you cannot take out what is inside the gourd with your hand or with a spoon.

ref: Let the Lord lead you and trust him to help.
text: Pʋ fʋ ɩwɩ wa Nana Bulu ɩbɩtɔ, afʋpʋ ansɩ dɩnka mʋsʋ, ɔbɔ́bwɛ mʋ́ ha fʋ.
translated: If you go to Nana Bulu’s house, do not make noise, or he will be angry with you.

ref: but the poor will take the land and enjoy a big harvest.
text: Ɩwɩasɩ abapʋ obégyi ɔsʋlʋ amʋ, ɩwɩ bɛ́hɩɛ lwii amʋ́.
translated: If you give someone a fish, they will eat for a day.

ref: The wicked will lose all of their power, but the Lord gives strength to everyone who is good.
text: Nana Bulu tɛkpa lalahɛ ɔbwɛpʋ ɩba, tamɛ ɔtɔwa yile ɔbwɛpʋ ɔwʋnlɩn.
translated: Nana Bulu took the sheep home, but the goat remained outside.

ref: An evil person borrows and never pays back; a good person is generous and never stops giving.
text: Ɔha lalahɛ ɔpan ɔtamaka, tamɛ yile ɔbwɛpʋ mʋ́ bʋ awɩtɔlɛ, otekie atɔ.
translated: The chief sat on the stool, and the people gathered under the big tree, listening quietly.

ref: When you are angry, Lord, please don't punish me or even correct me.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, mápʋ ɔblɔ wa ɩyɩn wa mɩ! Mápʋ ansɩpɛpɛ bɩtɩ mɩ ɩsʋ!
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, help me and my child! Help us so that we do not die!

ref: My body is twisted and bent, and I groan all day long.
text: Naklo, ndɛ ipian wun. Nawa nwɛ, ndesu ekekegyiɛkɛ.
translated: Come, let us go home. When we arrive, we will eat together.

ref: You, Lord, know every one of my deepest desires, and my noisy groans are no secret to you.
text: Mɩ Wie, fʋyin tɔá ndedunka. Tɔá sʋá ndesu wa ɩwɩtɔ ɩda fʋ ansɩtɔ.
translated: My friend, please bring the book. Bring it here so that I can read it to you.

ref: Because of my sickness, no friends or neighbors will come near me.
text: Mɩ ɩlɔ sʋ mɩ adwɛpʋ pʋ́ mɩ anyawie basrɩ mɩ. Mɩ abusuanfɔ onuto kʋraa bawa ɩfɔ mɩ ɩwɩ.
translated: Yesterday I went to my farm and I worked hard. My younger sibling also came with me.

ref: I told you my sins, and I am sorry for them.
text: Nalɛ mɩ lakpan blɩ. Ɩlahɔ mɩ ɔsɩn ánɩ́ nabwɛ mʋ́.
translated: Yesterday I went to the farm. My mother gave me food there.

ref: I felt a fire burning inside, and the more I thought, the more it burned, until at last I said:
text: Mɩ klʋn lɛblɩ ogya wa mɩ ɩwɩtɔ pɩnpɩɩnpɩn. Brɛá ndɛ mʋ́ ɩwɩ gywɩɩn kotoo a, iletsii ogya. Mʋ́ sʋ nɛblɩ mbɛɛ,
translated: When I put fire under the pot, it becomes very hot. If I remove the fire, the pot cools down. That is how it happens.

ref: “Please, Lord, show me my future. Will I soon be gone?
text: “Nana Bulu, suna mɩ mɩ nkpa ɔfʋnkpa, pʋ́ nkɛá ɩlasi mɩ. Suna mɩ alɩá mɩ lowu bɔ́wa ɔsa.
translated: Nana Bulu, my father and my friend, you are always with me. You are my strength and my hope every day.

ref: Save me from my sins. Don't let fools sneer at me.
text: Lɛ mɩ tsu mɩ lakpan fɛ́ɛ́tɔ. Mási mɩ ha, aha mimlahɛ abʋmɔsɩ mɩ.
translated: If you give me my book, I will be happy, because I have been looking for it for a long time.

ref: “Listen, Lord, to my prayer! My eyes are flooded with tears, as I pray to you. I am merely a stranger visiting in your home as my ancestors did.
text: “Oo Nana Bulu, nu mɩ mpaɩ, afʋnu mɩ isu. Mákina nu. Tsufɛ ɔfɔɔ ngyi, ndɛka tsʋn kɛkɛ fɛ mɩ anain.
translated: Yes, Nana Bulu, I am tired, my body is weak. I want to sleep. Please sit here, let me tell you something before I sleep.

ref: You, Lord God, have done many wonderful things, and you have planned marvelous things for us. No one is like you! I would never be able to tell all you have done.
text: Oo Nana Bulu, mɩ Bulu, ofula tsɔtsɔɔtsɔá fabwɛ ha mɩ, pʋ́ atɔá fahɩɛ ha mɩ lɔdʋbɩ. Tɔtɔ má ɩnʋá nɔ́pʋkapʋ fʋ. Nɩ mbɛɛ, mbwii ɔnɔ blɩ mʋ́ a, ɩma ɩklatɔ.
translated: Oh Nana Bulu, my Bulu, you have always cared for me, you have always protected me. Today I come to ask you for help. If you hear, accept my request, and help me.

ref: Our Lord, let your worshipers rejoice and be glad. They love you for saving them, so let them always say, “The Lord is wonderful!”
text: Tamɛ ha ɩwɩ ɩsankɩ aha ánɩ́ bʋtodunka fʋ fɛ́ɛ́, abʋnya fʋ ɩwɩ ansigyi. Ansɩ igyi aha ánɩ́ fʋ nkpahɔ legyi amʋ́ ansɩ, abʋkplʋn blɩ lablɩ bɛɛ, “Nana Bulu lomoni!”
translated: If you want to do something that will benefit you, first think about it carefully. If you think it will not help you in the future, leave it and say, "Nana Bulu knows!"

ref: My vicious enemies ask me, “When will you die and be forgotten?”
text: Mɩ alupʋ bʋtamablɩ mɩ yile dʋn “Ɔmɛnkɛ kʋraa obówu, mʋ ɩda bɔ́fwɩ?”
translated: When I was a child I used to hear the saying, “If the chief himself is a thief, who will judge the case?”

ref: When visitors come, all they ever bring are worthless words, and when they leave, they spread gossip.
text: Bʋtɛba bɛkɩ mɩ, fɛ amʋ́ ba. Tamɛ bɔyɔ a, bapʋ mɩ ɩwɩ asʋn lalahɛ bɛya.
translated: If the elders agree with me, then I will go. When it is morning, they will call me to come to the meeting place.

ref: Then my enemies won't defeat me, and I will know that you really care.
text: Ɩlaha nawun ánɩ́ fʋtekle mɩ asʋn, tsufɛ fʋmɛ́ɛha mɩ alupʋ abʋnya mɩ ɩwɩ ansigyi.
translated: If you want to get something good, you must work hard and be patient.

ref: You have helped me because I am innocent, and you will always be close to my side.
text: Ɔlala ɩma mɩ ɩwɩ, sʋ fatsu mɩ lɩɩ tɩntɩn. Faha ansɩ dɛ mɩgyi fʋ ansɩtɔ brɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: My friend came to me, and we talked for a long time. Then he said he would come again tomorrow morning.

ref: Why am I discouraged? Why am I restless? I should trust you, Lord. I will praise you again because you help me,
text: Mɩ ɔkla, ntogyi sʋ asʋn dɛ fʋ han alɩ? Ntogyi sʋ fawa nwɛ alɩ? Pʋ ansɩ dɩnka Bulusʋ, tsufɛ nɛ́lakanfʋ mɩ bʋalɛ ogyipʋ
translated: My friend, where are you coming from this morning? Where are you going this afternoon? I am asking because Bulus told me that you were looking for me yesterday.

ref: Why am I discouraged? Why am I restless? I trust you, Lord! And I will praise you again because you help me, and you are my God.
text: Mɩ ɔkla, ntogyi sʋ asʋn dɛ fʋ han alɩ? Ntogyi sʋ fawa nwɛ alɩ? Pʋ ansɩ dɩnka Bulusʋ, tsufɛ nɛ́lakanfʋ mɩ bʋalɛ ogyipʋ pʋ́ mɩ Bulu.
translated: My friend, why are you sad today? Why are you not happy? When I heard about Bulus, my heart was troubled and I was not happy.

ref: I run to you for protection. Why do you turn me away? Why must enemies mistreat me and make me sad?
text: Tsufɛ fʋgyi mɩ Bulu pʋ́ mɩ ɔtanʋkpa. “Ntogyi sʋ fasi mɩ wa ɔkpa? Ntogyi sʋ mɩ alupʋ bʋdɩn mɩsʋ, mʋ́ sʋ nɔ́wa nwɛ natɩ tsʋntsʋn?”
translated: The Hare asked Bulu, “Why are you always angry with me? Why do you always close your mouth at me, and you never want to talk to me?”

ref: You chased off the nations by causing them trouble with your powerful arm. Then you let our ancestors take over their land.
text: Fɔpʋ ɩbɩ gya ɔmá-ɔmá, tamɛ fɛha anɩ anain mʋ́ bɛlɛ nlin. Fobwie ɔmá-ɔmá, tamɛ fɛha amʋ́ mʋ́ bɛklɛɩ.
translated: When you are young, you eat and drink whatever you want. When you are old, you eat whatever you get.

ref: You sold your people for little or nothing, and you earned no profit.
text: Fafɛ fʋ aha ibia pɔnhɛ, fʋmenya tɔtɔ anɩsʋ.
translated: If you wake up early in the morning, you will get good things.

ref: You would have known it because you discover every secret thought.
text: Bulu, tɛkɩ fʋmɛ́ɛbɩ? Tsufɛ fʋyin anɩ agywɩɩntɔ asʋn ŋainhɛ kugyikʋ.
translated: Friend, how are you? Please help me with some money to buy food.

ref: We face death all day for you. We are like sheep on their way to be slaughtered.
text: Ee! Fʋ sʋ anɩtefia lowu ɔpa-onye, fɛ akufa bapʋ bɛya ɔmɔkpa.
translated: Yes! If you see a chief coming, even the dead will rise up.

ref: Mighty king, glorious ruler, strap on your sword
text: Otumipʋ, tsu fʋ ɔdayi tsan. Kɩta fʋ numnyam pʋ́ fʋ iwiegyitɔ.
translated: Almighty, give us your grace. Cover us with your loving kindness.

ref: You love justice and hate evil. And so, your God chose you and made you happier than any of your friends.
text: Fʋtekle yilebwɛ. Lalahɛbwɛ mʋ́ a, fʋtekisi. Mʋ́ sʋ fʋ Wie Bulu lada fʋ ofuli, pʋ ansigyi bʋla fʋtɔ dʋn fʋ aba.
translated: Morning has come. If you wake up, wash your face. If you do not wash your face before you eat, you will not be able to see well throughout the day.

ref: The sweet aroma of the spices myrrh, aloes, and cassia covers your royal robes. You enjoy the music of harps in palaces decorated with ivory.
text: Ohuam, pɩprɩ, ɔhɩɛn pʋ́ atɔ fanfan ɩdɛ ɔfan wa fʋ atadɩɛ tɩntɩntɔ fanfaanfan. Owie woyiá bɔpʋ srʋfɔ-ɩsɛn hɩhɩɛ mʋ́tɔ, ndondo aná dɛ mʋ́tɔ tsia bʋ fʋ ɔdwɛ.
translated: Yesterday, a goat, a sheep, and a cow went to the farm and each of them carried a basket full of tomatoes. When they returned, they met a hunter on the way, who asked them what they were carrying.

ref: Daughters of kings are here, and your bride stands at your right side, wearing a wedding gown trimmed with pure gold.
text: Awie abi tsɩhɛ bʋbʋ fʋ akatɔ. Ɔtsɩá owie detsia lɔwa Ofir sika atɔ wanklaan, ɔlɩɩ fʋ gyɔpɩsʋ.
translated: After that, he bought a field for you. He took gold from Ophir to buy the land, and gave it to you as a gift.

ref: The king adores you. He is your master, so do what he desires.
text: mɛnɩ owie ansɩ bégyi fʋ ɩwɩ. Fʋ wie nɩ. Mʋ́ sʋ ba ɩwɩasɩ ha mʋ.
translated: When you finish, come and take your food. Your food is here. They said I should give it to you.

ref: Your sons and your grandsons will also be kings as your ancestors were. You will make them rulers everywhere on earth.
text: Nanain, fʋ abi yinhɛ bétsia fʋ asɩ́ mbiasʋ. Fɛ́yaɩ amʋ́ awie ɔmá-ɔmátɔ.
translated: My friend, I told you that you should take care of yourself. That is why I always advise you.

ref: God is our mighty fortress, always ready to help in times of trouble.
text: Bulu gyi anɩ okpikplikpa pʋ́ anɩ ɔwʋnlɩn. Ɔma ɔka anɩ wa anɩ ɔkplankɛ.
translated: The goat went to the river to drink water. It saw a dog and greeted it.

ref: The Lord All-Powerful is with us. The God of Jacob is our fortress.
text: Ɩsa Ɔnɔ Ɔlɩɩpʋ Nana Bulu bʋ anɩ wa. Yakob Bulu amʋ gyi anɩ ɔtanʋkpa kinkin. Ɩya.
translated: It was Nana Bulu who first arrived here. Yakob Bulu was very brave and strong. That is all.

ref: The Lord Most High is fearsome, the ruler of all the earth.
text: tsufɛ Nana Bulu, Ɔsʋsʋʋsʋ mʋ Wie amʋ ɩwɩ bʋ ifu. Owie kpɔnkpɔɔnkpɔntɩ ogyi ɔyɩ fɛ́ɛ́sʋ.
translated: Nana Bulu died, and his people mourned greatly. The whole town was filled with deep sorrow.

ref: God is there to defend it and has proved to be its protector.
text: Bulu onuto bʋ awie amʋ woyitɔ. Alalɛ ɩwɩ ɔwan ánɩ́ mʋgyi ɔtanʋkpa kinkin ha mʋ́.
translated: The dog went to the river to drink water. There, it saw a man who was washing his big black cloth.

ref: Our God, here in your temple we think about your love.
text: Oo Bulu, anɩdɛ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka ɩwɩ gywɩɩn fʋ ɔtswɛkpa ɩnʋ.
translated: But Bulu, you should not say that you are better than your friend.

ref: We will see its strong walls and visit each fortress. Then you can say to future generations,
text: Mlɩlɛ ansɩ kɩ mʋ́ abunti wanklaan. Mlɩkɩ awie-woyi amʋ wanklaan. Fówun mlɔ́pʋbla mlɩ abi-aná mlɩaa,
translated: They said that I should not go outside at night. They told my siblings and me not to go outside. If we disobeyed, I would be punished.

ref: Everyone on this earth, now listen to what I say!
text: Mlɩ ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́, mlɩnu asʋn anfɩ! Ɔyɩtɔ atsiapʋ fɛ́ɛ́, mlɩyaa asʋ.
translated: If I say yes, I will be in trouble! If he asks again, I will run.

ref: Listen, no matter who you are, rich or poor.
text: Nyebi-ahandɛ, ɩwɩ anyapʋ pʋ́ ahianfɔ fɛ́ɛ́!
translated: Hello, I have brought you some good news!

ref: I have in mind a mystery that I will explain while playing my harp.
text: Nɛ́yaa asʋ nu ayebi. Nɔ́pʋ ɔsankuo lɛ ɔtɛ kutuklu asɩ.
translated: He/she has eaten the food. The bird is sitting on the tree branch.

ref: You cannot buy back your life or pay off God!
text: Ɔhaa mɛ́ɛtalɩ ka lɛ mʋ pio. Ɔhaa mɛ́ɛtalɩ ka Bulu ɩkɔ, pʋhɔ mʋ pio nkpa.
translated: If I go, I will see him. If I go to Bulu, maybe I will see him there.

ref: Soon they will die and all their wealth will be left behind.
text: Tsufɛ nɩ owu a, omóotsu kʋkʋ natɩ. Mʋ numnyam ɛ ɩmɛ́ɛkpa mʋ ya.
translated: When night falls, the children go to sleep. I am happy because of that.

ref: God shines brightly from Zion, the most beautiful city.
text: Bulu tɔwankɩ tsu Sion, wuluá ɩbʋ akɩlɛ ɔyɩtɔ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: The rain falls on Zion, its water flows everywhere.

ref: “Call my followers together! They offered me a sacrifice, and we made an agreement.”
text: Ɔbɛɛ, “Mlɩkpa aha ánɩ́ bʋtamakpa mɩ ɔma wa abatɔ ha mɩ. Aha amʋʋ bʋtɔbɔ afɔdɩɛ, ka ntam ha mɩ amʋ!”
translated: He said, “Do not be afraid but listen to what I am about to tell you. I am not bringing you trouble, so do not be afraid!”

ref: You made friends with every crook you met, and you liked people who break their wedding vows.
text: Fowun owikplu fakpla buo mʋ. Fʋa mbʋa atɔpʋ bʋ mba.
translated: The woman is pounding fufu in the room. She is using a big mortar and pestle.

ref: you sat around gossiping, ruining the reputation of your own relatives.”
text: Fʋtetsia asɩ blɩ fʋ pio ɩwɩ asʋn. Fʋtɛtalɩ kpɔɩ fʋ yin mʋ bi kʋraa.
translated: You should not eat with dirty hands. You must always wash your hands before eating.

ref: When you did all this, I didn't say a word, and you thought, “God is just like us!” But now I will accuse you.
text: Ɩnɩá fʋdɛ ntobi anfɩ fɛ́ɛ́ bwɛ mmɛkpla fʋ sʋ a, fʋkɩ fɛɛ, ngyi fɛ fʋ. Nɛ́lɛ mʋ́ fɛ́ɛ́ blɩ, wa ɩyɩn wa fʋ ngya.”
translated: If you want to get something good, you must work for it. If you just sit and wait, you will get nothing.

ref: The sacrifice that honors me is a thankful heart. Obey me, and I, your God, will show my power to save.
text: Ɔha ánɩ́ ɔtɔpʋ ɩpan bɔ afɔdɩɛ ha mɩ lawa mɩ numnyam. Ɔha ánɩ́ ɔna yilebwɛ ɔkpasʋ a, mʋ nɔ́hɔ nkpa.”
translated: If someone does something good for you, you should remember it. If someone does something bad to you, forget it.

ref: You are kind, God! Please have pity on me. You are always merciful! Please wipe away my sins.
text: Oo Bulu, fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka sʋ wa awɩtɔlɛ ha mɩ. Fʋ nwewun sʋ tsitsi mɩ lalahɛ.
translated: Mr. Bulu, please tell me if you have finished the work. Let me know so that I can come.

ref: Let me be happy and joyful! You crushed my bones, now let them celebrate.
text: Ha mɩ annu asʋn ánɩ́ ɩbɛ́ha ansɩ bégyi mɩ. Ha ansɩ igyi mɩ awuá fabiabia.
translated: If you don't give me food, I will not help you. If you help me, I will be very happy.

ref: Turn your eyes from my sin and cover my guilt.
text: Bun ansibi mɩ lakpansʋ, afʋtsitsi mɩ lalahɛ fɛ́ɛ́.
translated: If you wake up early, you will finish your work quickly.

ref: Offerings and sacrifices are not what you want.
text: tsufɛ afɔdɩɛ tamagyi fʋ ansɩ. Nɩ nɔbɔ atɔtɔhɛ-afɔdɩɛ ha fʋ kʋraa a, fʋmedekle.
translated: If you do not do the right thing, you will not be happy. And if you do something wrong, you will suffer.

ref: You people may be strong and brag about your sins, but God can be trusted day after day.
text: Ɔha yinhɛ, ntogyi sʋ fʋdɛ ɩwɩ adabi da, lalahɛá fʋdɛpʋgyi Bulu aha sʋ? Ekekegyiɛkɛ ɔyɩkɛhɛ fɛ́ɛ́,
translated: If so, why do you still eat Bulu food, even though you say you don't like their customs? That is what I want to know.

ref: You plan brutal crimes, and your lying words cut like a sharp razor.
text: fʋdɛ lalahɛ susu. Ofunupʋ olembiapʋ, fʋ ɔdandʋ bʋ ogya fɛ ɔdayibi.
translated: The child is crying. His mother is not here, so he is afraid of the dark and the night.

ref: God will destroy you forever! He will grab you and drag you from your homes. You will be uprooted and left to die.
text: Tamɛ Bulu ɔbɛ́da fʋ lɛ da, fʋmɛ́ɛtra kʋsʋ ɛkɛkɛɛkɛ. Ɔbɛ́lɛ fʋ lɛ fʋ tati-obuto, puli fʋ lɛ akɩankpapʋ ɔsʋlʋsʋ. Ɩya.
translated: If Bulu sleeps in your house today, you should be careful. He will take your three-legged stool, and he will steal your precious beads. That's all.

ref: “Just look at them now! Instead of trusting God, they trusted their wealth and their cruelty.”
text: “Kɩ, ntɔ dɛ ɔha yinhɛ anfɩ bwɛ? Ɔtamasrɩ tanʋ Bulu, ɩnyasʋ ɔlɔpʋ ansɩ dɩnka, pʋ atonyahɛ bwɛ mʋ ɔtanʋkpa.”
translated: So, what did you see there yesterday? Tamasi went to Bulu, but he didn’t meet anyone, and he returned home in the evening.

ref: From heaven God looks down to see if anyone is wise enough to search for him.
text: Nana Bulu tɛkɩ anyankpʋsa fɛ́ɛ́ sɩsɩ tsu ɔsʋsʋ, kɩ bɛɛ, menya ɔtɩansɩpʋ ɔkʋá otodunka mʋ ɔkpa.
translated: Nana Bulu took the children to the farm early in the morning, and there, they found a tortoise under a big tree.

ref: Save me, God, by your power and prove that I am right.
text: Oo Bulu, pʋ fʋ ɩda hɔ mɩ, afʋpʋ fʋ ɔwʋnlɩn gyi mɩ asʋn ha mɩ.
translated: Oh Bulu, if you are there, please help me because I am in trouble.

ref: you will punish my enemies for their evil deeds. Be my faithful friend and destroy them.
text: Pʋ mɩ alupʋ lalahɛ ka amʋ́ ɩkɔ. Fʋbʋ ɔnɔkwalɩ ha mɩ, sʋ mɔ amʋ́.
translated: If you want to go to the farm, tell me so that I go with you.

ref: because my enemies attack with loud shouts. They treat me terribly and hold angry grudges.
text: mɩ alupʋ asʋn blɩhɛ pʋ́ aha lalahɛ asɩá bʋdɛ mɩ ba sʋ. Tsufɛ bapʋ ipian dɩnka mɩsʋ, mɩ ɩwɩ ɔblɔ laha bʋdɛ mɩ lu.
translated: When I was going to the farm yesterday, I met a friend on the way. After we talked for a while, I continued on my way to the farm.

ref: I would quickly find shelter from howling winds and raging storms.
text: Tɛkɩ nɔ́kɔsɩ dunka oŋainkpa ha ɩwɩ, si afu mʋ́a alankpla anfɩ.
translated: Take this medicine every morning for a week, and you will feel much better soon.

ref: Confuse my enemies, Lord! Upset their plans. Cruelty and violence are all I see in the city,
text: Mɩ Wie, ha amʋ́ abʋgyigya, amʋ́ ɔdandʋ imlimli, tsufɛ owuntɔlɩn mʋ́a ɩkɔ́ gyi tɔá ndewun wulu amʋtɔ.
translated: My child, if you are diligent, you will gain wisdom, just as a bee gathers honey from many flowers.

ref: But it was my closest friend, the one I trusted most.
text: Tamɛ fʋ mɩ ba, mɩ nyawie pʋ́ mɩ ɔbɩsʋfɛá nhɩɛ nyin fʋ, dɛpʋ ɩnɩ gyi mɩ.
translated: If you come to me, I will give you something that will help you, so do not be afraid of me.

ref: I ask for your help, Lord God, and you will keep me safe.
text: Mɩ mʋ́ nósu kpoli Bulu. Nana Bulu ɔbɔ́hɔ mɩ.
translated: I am going to Bulu’s farm. Nana Bulu is my grandfather.

ref: I praise your promises!
text: Bulu asʋn ntɛkanfʋ. Nana Bulu asʋn ntɛkanfʋ.
translated: Bulu is a good farmer. Nana Bulu is a good farmer.

ref: May you, my God, be honored above the heavens; may your glory be seen everywhere on earth.
text: Oo Bulu, tsu fʋ ɩwɩ fʋa dafa ɔsʋsʋ, afʋha fʋ numnyam ɩbʋla ɔsʋlʋsʋ fɛ́ɛ́!
translated: Hey Bulu, go and bring your book here, so that we can read it together!

ref: Do you mighty people talk only to oppose justice? Don't you ever judge fairly?
text: Atumipʋ, lɛlɛ mlɩtɛblɩ asʋn ánɩ́ ɩda ɔkpa? Mlɩtegyi asʋn ɔnɔkwalɩsʋ ha aha?
translated: Teacher, why did you not come to school yesterday? Did something happen at home or on the way?

ref: You are always planning evil, and you are brutal.
text: O-o, lalahɛ bʋ mlɩ ɩklʋntɔ. Owuntɔlɩnla mlɩtɔbwɛ ɔsʋlʋsʋ.
translated: Yes, today we are going to the farm. The children are going to fetch firewood.

ref: Your words spread poison like the bite of a cobra
text: Bʋbʋ elin fɛ ɩwɔ. Bʋgyi sapa ɔkʋá oletila asʋ.
translated: The goat is here. The dog is lying under the tree.

ref: My enemies are fierce as lions, Lord God! Shatter their teeth. Snatch out their fangs.
text: Oo Bulu, kpakpa lalahɛ abwɛpʋ anfɩ ayín. Oo Nana Bulu, tsii gyata-abi anfɩ ayín-kɩtatɔ!
translated: Oh Bulu, you are truly a brave man among men. Oh Nana Bulu, you are like a lion among men indeed!

ref: My enemies return at evening, growling like dogs roaming the city.
text: Bʋteyinki ba ntʋpwɛ, bʋdɛkplʋn fɛ akɩaɩ, bomli wulu amʋ.
translated: Children are playing, they are laughing loudly, everyone is happy.

ref: Those liars return at evening, growling like dogs roaming the city.
text: Bʋteyinki ba ntʋpwɛ, bʋdɛkplʋn fɛ akɩaɩ, bomli wulu amʋ.
translated: Children are playing, they are making noise, the house is full of joy.

ref: You made the earth shake and split wide open; now heal its wounds and stop its trembling.
text: Fʋlɛha ɔsʋlʋ lɛkpɩnkɩ, ɩlɛbaba. Yinkia mʋ́ tsa aba, tsufɛ idekle ɩmaɩn-maɩn.
translated: The child is crying, mother. Please come here, something is hurting him a lot.

ref: Help us defeat our enemies! No one else can rescue us.
text: Tsa anɩ agyi anɩ alupʋsʋ. Nyankpʋsa mbʋa mʋ́ kpalɩ igyi.
translated: Take your book and your pencil. The teacher will come and teach you.

ref: You are a strong tower, where I am safe from my enemies.
text: Tsufɛ fʋgyi mɩ ɔtanʋkpa, obu fʋahɛá iletri mɩ alupʋ sʋ.
translated: If you see a snake, do not throw a stone at it.

ref: I feel like a shaky fence or a sagging wall. How long will all of you attack and assault me?
text: Ɔmɛnkɛ mlési mɩ nkʋlɛ han? Mlɩ fɛ́ɛ́ mlɩdekleá mlɛ́lɛ mɩ ɩnʋ, fɛ bunti ntɛ ɩban bwiehɛ?
translated: Where will you sleep today? Will you come to my house, or will you stay with your friend?

ref: We humans are only a breath; none of us are truly great. All of us together weigh less than a puff of air.
text: Lɛlɛ, nyebi-ahandɛ bʋgyi fɛ afu. Ɩwɩ anyapʋ ɛ bʋmegyi tɔtɔ. Kali amʋ́ afʋkɩ. Amʋ́ aku anyɔ anfɩ fɛ́ɛ́ bʋgyi fɛ dantapu.
translated: Yesterday, the children went to the farm. They helped their mother to weed. They worked well. Their father was happy because the children worked hard.

ref: Don't trust in violence or depend on dishonesty or rely on great wealth.
text: Mápʋ ansɩ dɩnka adufudɩɛ atɔsʋ. Másusuá fénya tɔkʋ tsu owitɔ. Nɩ fʋ atɔ ɩklɛɩ kʋraa a, mápʋ ansɩ dɩnka mʋ́sʋ.
translated: If you do not buy the food, I will not cook. I am not a magician. If you bring only salt, and you do not buy anything else, I will not cook for you.

ref: Your love means more than life to me, and I praise you.
text: Fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka bʋ alɛ dʋn nkpa. Mʋ́ sʋ nɛ́kanfʋ fʋ.
translated: You should not eat food that does not belong to you. That is not good.

ref: I think about you, God, before I go to sleep, and my thoughts turn to you during the night.
text: Nɩ nda ɔklansʋ oo ntɛkaɩn fʋsʋ. Ntɔgywɩɩn fʋ ɩwɩ onye fɛ́ɛ́,
translated: When I went to the farm, I saw a snake. The snake was lying on the path.

ref: They are determined to do evil, and they tell themselves, “Let's set traps! No one can see us.”
text: Bʋdɛ amʋ́ agywɩɩn lalahɛtɔ kinkiinkin. Bʋtɛblɩá bɛ́fɩnɩ agbla ŋaintɔ, bʋtɛfɩtɛ ɩwɩ bɛɛ, “Ma obówun anɩ?”
translated: The goat was eating grass by the roadside. The sheep came and asked, "What are you doing?"

ref: Everyone will come to you because you answer prayer.
text: Mpaɩ onupʋ, fʋ wa anyankpʋsa fɛ́ɛ́ bɛ́ba.
translated: Don't cry, your elder sibling will come soon.

ref: When our sins get us down, you forgive us.
text: Anɩ lalahɛ legyi anɩsʋ, tamɛ fesikie anɩ.
translated: If you want to go, tell me first.

ref: You bless your chosen ones, and you invite them to live near you in your temple. We will enjoy your house, the sacred temple.
text: Ansɩ igyi ɔha ánɩ́ falɛ, kpɩa mʋ manta ɩwɩ, otsie fʋ wunsɩnɛsʋ. Fʋ woyitɔ, igyi fʋ onuto ɔtswɛkpa ɩnʋ atɔ wanklaan ɩbɔ́mwɛ anɩ.
translated: If you want to do good, first think about it, then act on your thoughts. If you are wise, your actions will bring you happiness and benefit others as well.

ref: You take care of the earth and send rain to help the soil grow all kinds of crops. Your rivers never run dry, and you prepare the earth to produce abundant grain.
text: Fʋtɛbɛkɩ ɔsʋlʋ, wʋlɩ ntsu mʋ́sʋ, ha mʋ́ ɩtɔwa nfɔ. Fʋtɛha ntsu tɔbʋla fʋ abʋnkpa, ɩtɛha amʋ́ atɔ-abi tɔwa alɛ. Tsufɛ alɩ fɛhɩɛ mʋ́ nɩ.
translated: The teacher is here, he is sitting on a chair, and he is holding a book. The children are standing in front of him, they are listening to what he is saying. Everyone in the room is quiet.

ref: All of you people, come praise our God! Let his praises be heard.
text: Ɔmá fɛ́ɛ́, mlɩkanfʋ anɩ Bulu. Mlɩkanfʋ mʋ aha abʋnu.
translated: He/she/it came early, I did not see Bulu. I did not see him/her at the river.

ref: I will bring sacrifices into your house, my God, and I will do what I promised
text: Nɔ́pʋ atɔtɔhɛ-afɔdɩɛ ba fʋ otsiakpa. Nɛ́ka ɩkɔá notsula ha fʋ,
translated: The child is sitting on the chair. The mother is looking at the child,

ref: when I was in trouble.
text: tɔá nobwii ɔnɔ blɩ; brɛá mbʋ ɩkplantɔ.
translated: he/she finished eating; then went to the farm.

ref: But God did listen and answered my prayer.
text: tamɛ lɛlɛ mʋ́ a, Bulu lanu mɩ isu, sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ.
translated: If you do it like that, Bulu will get angry with me, so I won't do it.

ref: But let your people be happy and celebrate because of you.
text: Tamɛ ha aha yile abʋgyi ɔdwɛ. Bʋfuli kpayi ansigyisʋ fʋ ansɩtɔ, hɩɛ nya ansigyi.
translated: We came here to look for medicine. The doctor said you are not sick, so there is no medicine.

ref: Your people settled there, and you were generous to everyone in need.
text: Fʋ aha bɔpʋ ɩnʋ bwɛ otsiakpa. Oo Bulu, fʋ awɩtɔlɛ sʋ fɔbʋla ahianfɔ.
translated: You should not eat this food. Oh Bulu, you must listen to your elders.

ref: You gave the command, Lord, and a chorus of women told what had happened:
text: Anɩ Wie lɛlɛ ɔnɔ, atsɩ tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ bɛblɩ asʋn wanklaan amʋ.
translated: When God speaks, his words bring peace to every troubled heart.

ref: “Kings and their armies retreated and ran, and everything they left is now being divided.
text: Bɛblɩ bɛɛ, “Ɩsa ɔnɔ alɩɩpʋ fɛ́ɛ́ bɛsrɩ!” Atsɩá bʋbʋ ɩnʋ beye atɔá besi bun.
translated: "They said, 'Let us go and help them!' So they all went together and arrived there quickly."

ref: God All-Powerful, you scattered the kings like snow falling on Mount Zalmon.
text: Brɛá Bulu Otumipʋ amʋ lɛda awie saɩn a, Salmonbʋsʋ lofuli fɛ dantapu.
translated: When King Bulu Otumipʋ finished his work, Salmonbʋsʋ sat quietly on the chair.

ref: You always protect us and save us from death.
text: Nkpa ɔhɔpʋ anɩ Bulu gyi. Anɩ Wie Nana Bulu tɛlɛ anɩ tsu lowutɔ.
translated: The chief is sitting with Bulu. And then Nana Bulu called his children.

ref: Our Lord and our God, your terrible enemies are ready for war, but you will crush their skulls.
text: Tamɛ obésisri mʋ alupʋ nwun. Amʋ́ na bʋdɛ ɩpɔngyila bwɛ nɩ.
translated: He/she will cook yam for you tomorrow. You should go and fetch firewood for him/her.

ref: The small tribe of Benjamin leads the way, followed by the leaders from Judah. Then come the leaders from Zebulun and Naphtali.
text: Amʋ́tɔ ɔtrahɛ Benyamin abusuan igyankpa, Yuda abusuan ahandɛ pʋ́ amʋ́ aha tsɔtsɔɔtsɔ bʋbuo, Sebulon mʋa Naftali abusuan ahandɛ ɛ bʋgya ɔma.
translated: The tribe of Benjamin is dead, the tribe of Judah is many but they are all broken pieces, the tribes of Zebulun and Naphtali are few and they are like a small fire.

ref: Force the Egyptians to bring gifts of bronze; make the Ethiopians hurry to offer presents.
text: Ha abʋpʋ kɔɔbrɩ tsua Egyipte ba. Etiopiafɔ abʋba ɩwɩasɩ ha fʋ Bulu.
translated: The ancestors first came from Egypt. The Ethiopians brought their language here to Bulu.

ref: The God of Israel is fearsome in his temple, and he makes us strong. Let's praise our God!
text: Bulu ɩwɩ bʋ ifu mʋ otsiakpa ɩnʋ. Israel Bulu tɔpʋ mʋ tumi mʋ́a ɔwʋnlɩn ha amʋ́. Mlɩkanfʋ Bulu!
translated: The king has done something very good. Israel's king has shown his power to everyone. Long live the king!

ref: You know my foolish sins. Not one is hidden from you.
text: Oo Bulu, fʋyin mɩ mimlatɔ. Mɩ lakpan meŋain fʋ.
translated: Mr. Bulu, give me your book. I want to read it.

ref: Lord God All-Powerful, ruler of Israel, don't let me embarrass anyone who trusts and worships you.
text: Oo anɩ Wie, Ɩsa Ɔnɔ Ɔlɩɩpʋ, Nana Bulu, mɩ sʋ máha pɛlɩ ɩkɩta fʋ ɔkpa akɩpʋ. Oo Israel Bulu, mɩ sʋ máha fʋ adunkapʋ abʋsʋpa.
translated: You are Wie, the God of Power, Nana Bulu, I have come to ask you for your help. You are the God of Israel, I have come to ask for your protection and blessings.

ref: My love for your house burns in me like a fire, and when others insult you, they insulted me as well.
text: Fʋ otsiakpa ɩnʋ asʋn ɩhɩɛ bʋ mɩ ogya. Fʋ asiabi fɛ́ɛ́ lɛbɛdɩnka mɩsʋ.
translated: If you want to cook food, you need fire. If you want to be successful, you must work hard.

ref: But I pray to you, Lord. So when the time is right, answer me and help me with your wonderful love.
text: Tamɛ mɩ mʋ́ fʋ Nana Bulu netsia su kpoli. Oo Bulu, brɛá iletsia fʋ a, pʋ fʋ ɔdwɛá ɩma ɔka pʋ́ fʋ nkpahɔá igyi ɔnɔkwalɩ sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ.
translated: If I were you, I would go to Nana Bulu for help. Oh Bulu, when someone comes to you, do not let them leave without giving them advice or helping them in some way.

ref: Don't let me be swept away by a flood or drowned in the ocean or swallowed by death.
text: Máha ntsu anfɩ ɩbʋla kɩta mɩ, ankpada wie ntsu-ibuntɔ, ntɛ lowu ɩpʋ mɩ.
translated: If you do not have anything to give me, just tell me the truth, and I will not be angry with you.

ref: Don't turn away from me. I am your servant, and I am in trouble. Please hurry and help!
text: Mápʋ ansɩtɔ ŋain fʋ osumbi. Ɩladʋn mɩ, wa ɔsa sɔ mɩ ɔnɔ.
translated: My father said you should come. After that, he will give you something.

ref: Enemies poisoned my food, and when I was thirsty, they gave me vinegar.
text: Brɛa akʋn dɛ mɩ a, bɔwa atɔ atɔtɔ ha mɩ bɛɛ, ngyi. Brɛá ɔmɛwʋlɩ dɛ mɩ a, bɔpʋ nta-ɔdaɩ ha mɩ bɛɛ nnun.
translated: If I have money, I will buy a goat, but I don't have any. If I have wisdom, I will give advice, but I don't have any.
